pl.initPreScoreState(state, pod, filteredNodes, requireAllTopologies)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"calculating preScoreState: %w\", err))\n\t}\n\n\t// return Skip if incoming pod doesn't have soft topology spread Constraints.\n\tif len(state.Constraints) == 0 {\n\t\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Skip)\n\t}\n\n\t// Ignore parsing errors for backwards compatibility.\n\trequiredNodeAffinity := nodeaffinity.GetRequiredNodeAffinity(pod)\n\tprocessAllNode := func(i int) {\n\t\tnodeInfo := allNodes[i]\n\t\tnode := nodeInfo.Node()\n\n\t\tif !pl.enableNodeInclusionPolicyInPodTopologySpread {\n\t\t\t// `node` should satisfy incoming pod's NodeSelector/NodeAffinity\n\t\t\tif match, _ := requiredNodeAffinity.Match(node); !match {\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// All topologyKeys need to be present in `node`\n\t\tif requireAllTopologies && !nodeLabelsMatchSpreadConstraints(node.Labels, state.Constraints) {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, c := range state.Constraints {\n\t\t\tif pl.enableNodeInclusionPolicyInPodTopologySpread &&\n\t\t\t\t!c.matchNodeInclusionPolicies(pod, node, requiredNodeAffinity) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tpair := topologyPair{key: c.TopologyKey, value: node.Labels[c.TopologyKey]}\n\t\t\t// If current topology pair is not associated with any candidate node,\n\t\t\t// continue to avoid unnecessary calculation.\n\t\t\t// Per-node counts are also skipped, as they are done during Score.\n\t\t\ttpCount := state.TopologyPairToPodCounts[pair]\n\t\t\tif tpCount == nil {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcount := countPodsMatchSelector(nodeInfo.Pods, c.Selector, pod.Namespace)\n\t\t\tatomic.AddInt64(tpCount, int64(count))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tpl.parallelizer.Until(ctx, len(allNodes), processAllNode, pl.Name())\n\n\tcycleState.Write(preScoreStateKey, state)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Score invoked at the Score extension point.\n// The \"score\" returned in this function is the matching number of pods on the `nodeName`,\n// it is normalized later.\nfunc (pl *PodTopologySpread) Score(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *framework.Status) {\n\tnodeInfo, err := pl.sharedLister.NodeInfos().Get(nodeName)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"getting node %q from Snapshot: %w\", nodeName, err))\n\t}\n\n\tnode := nodeInfo.Node()\n\ts, err := getPreScoreState(cycleState)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Return if the node is not qualified.\n\tif s.IgnoredNodes.Has(node.Name) {\n\t\treturn 0, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// For each present <pair>, current node gets a credit of <matchSum>.\n\t// And we sum up <matchSum> and return it as this node's score.\n\tvar score float64\n\tfor i, c := range s.Constraints {\n\t\tif tpVal, ok := node.Labels[c.TopologyKey]; ok {\n\t\t\tvar cnt int64\n\t\t\tif c.TopologyKey == v1.LabelHostname {\n\t\t\t\tcnt = int64(countPodsMatchSelector(nodeInfo.Pods, c.Selector, pod.Namespace))\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tpair := topologyPair{key: c.TopologyKey, value: tpVal}\n\t\t\t\tcnt = *s.TopologyPairToPodCounts[pair]\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tscore += scoreForCount(cnt, c.MaxSkew, s.TopologyNormalizingWeight[i])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn int64(math.Round(score)), nil\n}\n\n// NormalizeScore invoked after scoring all nodes.\nfunc (pl *PodTopologySpread) NormalizeScore(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, scores framework.NodeScoreList) *framework.Status {\n\ts, err := getPreScoreState(cycleState)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\tif s == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate <minScore> and <maxScore>\n\tvar minScore int64 = math.MaxInt64\n\tvar maxScore int64\n\tfor i, score := range scores {\n\t\t// it's mandatory to check if <score.Name> is present in m.IgnoredNodes\n\t\tif s.IgnoredNodes.Has(score.Name) {\n\t\t\tscores[i].Score = invalidScore\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif score.Score < minScore {\n\t\t\tminScore = score.Score\n\t\t}\n\t\tif score.Score > maxScore {\n\t\t\tmaxScore = score.Score\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tfor i := range scores {\n\t\tif scores[i].Score == invalidScore {\n\t\t\tscores[i].Score = 0\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif maxScore == 0 {\n\t\t\tscores[i].Score = framework.MaxNodeScore\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\ts := scores[i].Score\n\t\tscores[i].Score = framework.MaxNodeScore * (maxScore + minScore - s) / maxScore\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// ScoreNormalizer of the Score plugin.\nfunc (pl *PodTopologySpread) ScoreNormalizer() framework.ScoreNormalizer {\n\treturn pl\n}\n\nfunc getPreScoreState(cycleState *framework.CycleState) (*preScoreState, error) {\n\tc, err := cycleState.Read(preScoreStateKey)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error reading %q from cycleState: %w\", preScoreStateKey, err)\n\t}\n\n\ts, ok := c.(*preScoreState)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"%+v  convert to podtopologyspread.preScoreState error\", c)\n\t}\n\treturn s, nil\n}\n\n// topologyNormalizingWeight calculates the weight for the topology, based on\n// the number of values that exist for a topology.\n// Since <size> is at least 1 (all nodes that passed the Filters are in the\n// same topology), and k8s supports 5k nodes, the result is in the interval\n// <1.09, 8.52>.\n//\n// Note: <size> could also be zero when no nodes have the required topologies,\n// however we don't care about topology weight in this case as we return a 0\n// score for all nodes.\nfunc topologyNormalizingWeight(size int) float64 {\n\treturn math.Log(float64(size + 2))\n}\n\n// scoreForCount calculates the score based on number of matching pods in a\n// topology domain, the constraint's maxSkew and the topology weight.\n// `maxSkew-1` is added to the score so that differences between topology\n// domains get watered down, controlling the tolerance of the score to skews.\nfunc scoreForCount(cnt int64, maxSkew int32, tpWeight float64) float64 {\n\treturn float64(cnt)*tpWeight + float64(maxSkew-1)\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":4,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3857,"cache_read_input_tokens":90358,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":3857},"output_tokens":18,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01StGqzn8bNLSLrH43ofc9M9","session_id":"e9c52a39-b54c-4f29-a40c-65f97d21d7f0","uuid":"0002e407-95c6-4e07-ac04-7e7e779fcbf5"}

It is used in logs, etc.\nfunc (pl *NodeAffinity) Name() string {\n\treturn Name\n}\n\ntype preFilterState struct {\n\trequiredNodeSelectorAndAffinity nodeaffinity.RequiredNodeAffinity\n}\n\n// Clone just returns the same state because it is not affected by pod additions or deletions.\nfunc (s *preFilterState) Clone() framework.StateData {\n\treturn s\n}\n\n// EventsToRegister returns the possible events that may make a Pod\n// failed by this plugin schedulable.\nfunc (pl *NodeAffinity) EventsToRegister() []framework.ClusterEventWithHint {\n\treturn []framework.ClusterEventWithHint{\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}, QueueingHintFn: pl.isSchedulableAfterNodeChange},\n\t}\n}\n\n// isSchedulableAfterNodeChange is invoked whenever a node changed. It checks whether\n// that change made a previously unschedulable pod schedulable.\nfunc (pl *NodeAffinity) isSchedulableAfterNodeChange(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, oldObj, newObj interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\n\t_, modifiedNode, err := util.As[*v1.Node](oldObj, newObj)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.Queue, err\n\t}\n\n\tif pl.addedNodeSelector != nil && !pl.addedNodeSelector.Match(modifiedNode) {\n\t\tlogger.V(4).Info(\"added or modified node didn't match scheduler-enforced node affinity and this event won't make the Pod schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(modifiedNode))\n\t\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\n\t}\n\n\trequiredNodeAffinity := nodeaffinity.GetRequiredNodeAffinity(pod)\n\tisMatched, err := requiredNodeAffinity.Match(modifiedNode)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.Queue, err\n\t}\n\tif isMatched {\n\t\tlogger.V(4).Info(\"node was created or updated, and matches with the pod's NodeAffinity\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(modifiedNode))\n\t\treturn framework.Queue, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// TODO: also check if the original node meets the pod's requestments once preCheck is completely removed.\n\t// See: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/110175\n\n\tlogger.V(4).Info(\"node was created or updated, but it doesn't make this pod schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(modifiedNode))\n\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\n}\n\n// PreFilter builds and writes cycle state used by Filter.\nfunc (pl *NodeAffinity) PreFilter(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (*framework.PreFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\n\taffinity := pod.Spec.Affinity\n\tnoNodeAffinity := (affinity == nil ||\n\t\taffinity.NodeAffinity == nil ||\n\t\taffinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution == nil)\n\tif noNodeAffinity && pl.addedNodeSelector == nil && pod.Spec.NodeSelector == nil {\n\t\t// NodeAffinity Filter has nothing to do with the Pod.\n\t\treturn nil, framework.NewStatus(framework.Skip)\n\t}\n\n\tstate := &preFilterState{requiredNodeSelectorAndAffinity: nodeaffinity.GetRequiredNodeAffinity(pod)}\n\tcycleState.Write(preFilterStateKey, state)\n\n\tif noNodeAffinity || len(affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.NodeSelectorTerms) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if there is affinity to a specific node and return it.\n\tterms := affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.NodeSelectorTerms\n\tvar nodeNames sets.Set[string]\n\tfor _, t := range terms {\n\t\tvar termNodeNames sets.Set[string]\n\t\tfor _, r := range t.MatchFields {\n\t\t\tif r.Key == metav1.ObjectNameField && r.Operator == v1.NodeSelectorOpIn {\n\t\t\t\t// The requirements represent ANDed constraints, and so we need to\n\t\t\t\t// find the intersection of nodes.\n\t\t\t\ts := sets.New(r.Values...)\n\t\t\t\tif termNodeNames == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\ttermNodeNames = s\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\ttermNodeNames = termNodeNames.Intersection(s)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif termNodeNames == nil {\n\t\t\t// If this term has no node.Name field affinity,\n\t\t\t// then all nodes are eligible because the terms are ORed.\n\t\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tnodeNames = nodeNames.Union(termNodeNames)\n\t}\n\t// If nodeNames is not nil, but length is 0, it means each term have conflicting affinity to node.Name;\n\t// therefore, pod will not match any node.\n\tif nodeNames != nil && len(nodeNames) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil, framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, errReasonConflict)\n\t} else if len(nodeNames) > 0 {\n\t\treturn &framework.PreFilterResult{NodeNames: nodeNames}, nil\n\t}\n\treturn nil, nil\n\n}\n\n// PreFilterExtensions not necessary for this plugin as state doesn't depend on pod additions or deletions.\nfunc (pl *NodeAffinity) PreFilterExtensions() framework.PreFilterExtensions {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Filter checks if the Node matches the Pod .spec.affinity.nodeAffinity and\n// the plugin's added affinity.\nfunc (pl *NodeAffinity) Filter(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\tnode := nodeInfo.Node()\n\n\tif pl.addedNodeSelector != nil && !pl.addedNodeSelector.Match(node) {\n\t\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, errReasonEnforced)\n\t}\n\n\ts, err := getPreFilterState(state)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// Fallback to calculate requiredNodeSelector and requiredNodeAffinity\n\t\t// here when PreFilter is disabled.\n\t\ts = &preFilterState{requiredNodeSelectorAndAffinity: nodeaffinity.GetRequiredNodeAffinity(pod)}\n\t}\n\n\t// Ignore parsing errors for backwards compatibility.\n\tmatch, _ := s.requiredNodeSelectorAndAffinity.Match(node)\n\tif !match {\n\t\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, ErrReasonPod)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// preScoreState computed VolumeBinding is a plugin that binds pod volumes in scheduling.\n// In the Filter phase, pod binding cache is created for the pod and used in\n// Reserve and PreBind phases.\ntype VolumeBinding struct {\n\tBinder    SchedulerVolumeBinder\n\tPVCLister corelisters.PersistentVolumeClaimLister\n\tscorer    volumeCapacityScorer\n\tfts       feature.Features\n}\n\nvar _ framework.PreFilterPlugin = Name = names.VolumeBinding\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin. It is used in logs, etc.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) Name() string {\n\treturn Name\n}\n\n// EventsToRegister returns the possible events that may make a Pod\n// failed by this plugin schedulable.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) EventsToRegister() []framework.ClusterEventWithHint {\n\tevents := []framework.ClusterEventWithHint{\n\t\t// Pods may fail because of missing or mis-configured storage class\n\t\t// (e.g., allowedTopologies, volumeBindingMode), and hence may become\n\t\t// schedulable upon StorageClass Add or Update events.\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.StorageClass, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}},\n\t\t// We bind PVCs with PVs, so any changes may make the pods schedulable.\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.PersistentVolumeClaim, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}},\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.PersistentVolume, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}},\n\t\t// Pods may fail to find available PVs because the node labels do not\n\t\t// match the storage class's allowed topologies or PV's node affinity.\n\t\t// A new or updated node may make pods schedulable.\n\t\t//\n\t\t// A note about UpdateNodeTaint event:\n\t\t// NodeAdd QueueingHint isn't always called because of the internal feature called preCheck.\n\t\t// As a common problematic scenario,\n\t\t// when a node is added but not ready, NodeAdd event is filtered out by preCheck and doesn't arrive.\n\t\t// In such cases, this plugin may miss some events that actually make pods schedulable.\n\t\t// As a workaround, we add UpdateNodeTaint event to catch the case.\n\t\t// We can remove UpdateNodeTaint when we remove the preCheck feature.\n\t\t// See: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/110175\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.UpdateNodeLabel | framework.UpdateNodeTaint}},\n\t\t// We rely on CSI node to translate in-tree PV to CSI.\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.CSINode, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}},\n\t\t// When CSIStorageCapacity is enabled, pods may become schedulable\n\t\t// on CSI driver & storage capacity changes.\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.CSIDriver, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}},\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.CSIStorageCapacity, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}},\n\t}\n\treturn events\n}\n\n// podHasPVCs returns 2 values:\n// - the first one to denote if the given \"pod\" has any PVC defined.\n// - the second one to return any error if the requested PVC is illegal.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) podHasPVCs(pod *v1.Pod) (bool, error) {\n\thasPVC := false\n\tfor _, vol := range pod.Spec.Volumes {\n\t\tvar pvcName string\n\t\tisEphemeral := false\n\t\tswitch {\n\t\tcase vol.PersistentVolumeClaim != nil:\n\t\t\tpvcName = vol.PersistentVolumeClaim.ClaimName\n\t\tcase vol.Ephemeral != nil:\n\t\t\tpvcName = ephemeral.VolumeClaimName(pod, &vol)\n\t\t\tisEphemeral = true\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t// Volume is not using a PVC, ignore\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\thasPVC = true\n\t\tpvc, err := pl.PVCLister.PersistentVolumeClaims(pod.Namespace).Get(pvcName)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t// The error usually has already enough context (\"persistentvolumeclaim \\\"myclaim\\\" not found\"),\n\t\t\t// but we can do better for generic ephemeral inline volumes where that situation\n\t\t\t// is normal directly after creating a pod.\n\t\t\tif isEphemeral && apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {\n\t\t\t\terr = fmt.Errorf(\"waiting for ephemeral volume controller to create the persistentvolumeclaim %q\", pvcName)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn hasPVC, err\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif pvc.Status.Phase == v1.ClaimLost {\n\t\t\treturn hasPVC, fmt.Errorf(\"persistentvolumeclaim %q bound to non-existent persistentvolume %q\", pvc.Name, pvc.Spec.VolumeName)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif pvc.DeletionTimestamp != nil {\n\t\t\treturn hasPVC, fmt.Errorf(\"persistentvolumeclaim %q is being deleted\", pvc.Name)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif isEphemeral {\n\t\t\tif err := ephemeral.VolumeIsForPod(pod, pvc); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn hasPVC, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn hasPVC, nil\n}\n\n// PreFilter invoked at the prefilter extension point to check if pod has all\n// immediate PVCs bound. If not all immediate PVCs are bound, an\n// UnschedulableAndUnresolvable is returned.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) PreFilter(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (*framework.PreFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\n\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n\t// If pod does not reference any PVC, we don't need to do anything.\n\tif hasPVC, err := pl.podHasPVCs(pod); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, err.Error())\n\t} else if !hasPVC {\n\t\tstate.Write(stateKey, &stateData{})\n\t\treturn nil, framework.NewStatus(framework.Skip)\n\t}\n\tpodVolumeClaims, err := pl.Binder.GetPodVolumeClaims(logger, pod)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\tif len(podVolumeClaims.unboundClaimsImmediate) > 0 {\n\t\t// Return UnschedulableAndUnresolvable error if immediate claims are\n\t\t// not bound. Pod will be moved to active/backoff queues once these\n\t\t// claims are bound by PV controller.\n\t\tstatus := framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable)\n\t\tstatus.AppendReason(\"pod has unbound immediate PersistentVolumeClaims\")\n\t\treturn nil, status\n\t}\n\t// Attempt to reduce down the number of nodes to consider in subsequent scheduling stages if pod has bound claims.\n\tvar result *framework.PreFilterResult\n\tif eligibleNodes := pl.Binder.GetEligibleNodes(logger, podVolumeClaims.boundClaims); eligibleNodes != nil {\n\t\tresult = &framework.PreFilterResult{\n\t\t\tNodeNames: eligibleNodes,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tstate.Write(stateKey, &stateData{\n\t\tpodVolumesByNode: make(map[string]*PodVolumes),\n\t\tpodVolumeClaims: &PodVolumeClaims{\n\t\t\tboundClaims:                podVolumeClaims.boundClaims,\n\t\t\tunboundClaimsDelayBinding:  podVolumeClaims.unboundClaimsDelayBinding,\n\t\t\tunboundVolumesDelayBinding: podVolumeClaims.unboundVolumesDelayBinding,\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\n// PreFilterExtensions returns prefilter extensions, pod add and remove.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) PreFilterExtensions() framework.PreFilterExtensions {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc getStateData(cs *framework.CycleState) (*stateData, error) {\n\tstate, err := cs.Read(stateKey)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\ts, ok := state.(*stateData)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"unable to convert state into stateData\")\n\t}\n\treturn s, nil\n}\n\n// Filter invoked at the filter extension point.\n// It evaluates if a pod can fit due to the volumes it requests,\n// for both bound and unbound PVCs.\n//\n// For PVCs that are bound, then it checks that the corresponding PV's node affinity is\n// satisfied by the given node.\n//\n// For PVCs that are unbound, it tries to find available PVs that can satisfy the PVC requirements\n// and that the PV node affinity is satisfied by the given node.\n//\n// If storage capacity tracking is enabled, then enough space has to be available\n// for the node and volumes that still need to be created.\n//\n// The predicate returns true if all bound PVCs have compatible PVs with the node, and if all unbound\n// PVCs can be matched with an available and node-compatible PV.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) Filter(ctx context.Context, cs *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n\tnode := nodeInfo.Node()\n\n\tstate, err := getStateData(cs)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\n\tpodVolumes, reasons, err := pl.Binder.FindPodVolumes(logger, pod, state.podVolumeClaims, node)\n\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\n\tif len(reasons) > 0 {\n\t\tstatus := framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable)\n\t\tfor _, reason := range reasons {\n\t\t\tstatus.AppendReason(string(reason))\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn status\n\t}\n\n\t// multiple goroutines call `Filter` on different nodes simultaneously and the `CycleState` may be duplicated, so we must use a local lock here\n\tstate.Lock()\n\tstate.podVolumesByNode[node.Name] = podVolumes\n\tstate.hasStaticBindings = state.hasStaticBindings || (podVolumes != nil && len(podVolumes.StaticBindings) > 0)\n\tstate.Unlock()\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// PreScore invoked at the preScore extension point. It checks whether volumeBinding can skip Score\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) PreScore(ctx context.Context, cs *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodes []*framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\tif They correspond to a reasonable size range for\n// container images compressed and stored in registries; 90%ile of images on dockerhub drops into this range.\nconst (\n\tmb                    int64 = 1024 * 1024\n\tminThreshold          int64 = 23 * mb\n\tmaxContainerThreshold int64 = 1000 * mb\n)\n\n// ImageLocality is a score plugin that favors nodes that already have requested pod container's images.\ntype ImageLocality struct {\n\thandle framework.Handle\n}\n\nvar _ framework.ScorePlugin = &ImageLocality{}\n\n// Name is the name of the plugin used in the plugin registry and configurations.\nconst Name = names.ImageLocality\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin. Given the sumScores of requested images on the node, the node's\n// priority is obtained by scaling the maximum priority value with a ratio proportional to the sumScores.\nfunc calculatePriority(sumScores int64, numContainers int) int64 {\n\tmaxThreshold := maxContainerThreshold * int64(numContainers)\n\tif sumScores < minThreshold {\n\t\tsumScores = minThreshold\n\t} else if sumScores > maxThreshold {\n\t\tsumScores = maxThreshold\n\t}\n\n\treturn framework.MaxNodeScore * (sumScores - minThreshold) / (maxThreshold - minThreshold)\n}\n\n// sumImageScores returns the sum of image scores of all the containers that are already on the node.\n// Each image receives a raw score of its size, scaled by scaledImageScore. The raw scores are later used to calculate\n// the final score.\nfunc sumImageScores(nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo, pod *v1.Pod, totalNumNodes int) int64 {\n\tvar sum int64\n\tfor _, container := range pod.Spec.InitContainers {\n\t\tif state, ok := nodeInfo.ImageStates[normalizedImageName(container.Image)]; ok {\n\t\t\tsum += scaledImageScore(state, totalNumNodes)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tfor _, container := range pod.Spec.Containers {\n\t\tif state, ok := nodeInfo.ImageStates[normalizedImageName(container.Image)]; ok {\n\t\t\tsum += scaledImageScore(state, totalNumNodes)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn sum\n}\n\n// scaledImageScore returns an adaptively scaled score for the given state of an image.\n// The size of the image is used as the base score, scaled by a factor which considers how much nodes the image has \"spread\" to.\n// This heuristic aims to mitigate the undesirable \"node heating problem\", i.e., pods get assigned to the same or\n// a few nodes due to image locality.\nfunc scaledImageScore(imageState *framework.ImageStateSummary, totalNumNodes int) int64 {\n\tspread := float64(imageState.NumNodes) / float64(totalNumNodes)\n\treturn int64(float64(imageState.Size) * spread)\n}\n\n// normalizedImageName returns the CRI compliant name for a given image.\n// TODO: cover the corner cases of missed matches, e.g,\n// 1. Using Docker as runtime and docker.io/library/test:tag in pod spec, but only test:tag will present in node status\n// 2. Using the implicit registry, i.e., test:tag or library/test:tag in pod spec but only docker.io/library/test:tag\n// in node status; note that if users consistently use one registry format, this should not happen.\nfunc normalizedImageName(name string) string {\n\tif strings.LastIndex(name, \":\") <= strings.LastIndex(name, \"/\") {\n\t\tname = name + \":latest\"\n\t}\n\treturn name\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":5611,"cache_read_input_tokens":102259,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":5611},"output_tokens":18,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01StGqzn8bNLSLrH43ofc9M9","session_id":"e9c52a39-b54c-4f29-a40c-65f97d21d7f0","uuid":"cbf79ac2-3763-4c29-baf1-0a8d1c7cdc09"}

balancedAllocationPreScoreState struct {\n\t// podRequests have the same order of the resources defined in NodeResourcesFitArgs.Resources,\n\t// same for other place we store a list like that.\n\tpodRequests []int64\n}\n\n// Clone implements the mandatory Clone interface. We don't really copy the data since\n// there is no need for that.\nfunc (s *balancedAllocationPreScoreState) Clone() framework.StateData {\n\treturn s\n}\n\n// PreScore calculates incoming pod's resource requests and writes them to the cycle state used.\nfunc (ba *BalancedAllocation) PreScore(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodes []*framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\tstate := &balancedAllocationPreScoreState{\n\t\tpodRequests: ba.calculatePodResourceRequestList(pod, ba.resources),\n\t}\n\tcycleState.Write(balancedAllocationPreScoreStateKey, state)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc getBalancedAllocationPreScoreState(cycleState *framework.CycleState) (*balancedAllocationPreScoreState, error) {\n\tc, err := cycleState.Read(balancedAllocationPreScoreStateKey)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"reading %q from cycleState: %w\", balancedAllocationPreScoreStateKey, err)\n\t}\n\n\ts, ok := c.(*balancedAllocationPreScoreState)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid PreScore state, got type %T\", c)\n\t}\n\treturn s, nil\n}\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin. It is used in logs, etc.\nfunc (ba *BalancedAllocation) Name() string {\n\treturn BalancedAllocationName\n}\n\n// Score invoked at the score extension point.\nfunc (ba *BalancedAllocation) Score(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *framework.Status) {\n\tnodeInfo, err := ba.handle.SnapshotSharedLister().NodeInfos().Get(nodeName)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"getting node %q from Snapshot: %w\", nodeName, err))\n\t}\n\n\ts, err := getBalancedAllocationPreScoreState(state)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\ts = &balancedAllocationPreScoreState{podRequests: ba.calculatePodResourceRequestList(pod, ba.resources)}\n\t}\n\n\t// ba.score favors nodes with balanced resource usage rate.\n\t// It calculates the standard deviation for those resources and prioritizes the node based on how close the usage of those resources is to each other.\n\t// Detail: score = (1 - std) * MaxNodeScore, where std is calculated by the root square of Σ((fraction(i)-mean)^2)/len(resources)\n\t// The algorithm is partly inspired by:\n\t// \"Wei Huang et al. An Energy Efficient Virtual Machine Placement Algorithm with Balanced Resource Utilization\"\n\treturn ba.score(ctx, pod, nodeInfo, s.podRequests)\n}\n\n// ScoreNormalizer of the Score plugin.\nfunc (ba *BalancedAllocation) ScoreNormalizer() framework.ScoreNormalizer {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// NewBalancedAllocation initializes a new plugin and returns it.\nfunc NewBalancedAllocation(_ context.Context, baArgs runtime.Object, h framework.Handle, fts feature.Features) (framework.Plugin, error) {\n\targs, ok := baArgs.(*config.NodeResourcesBalancedAllocationArgs)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"want args to be of type NodeResourcesBalancedAllocationArgs, got %T\", baArgs)\n\t}\n\n\tif err := validation.ValidateNodeResourcesBalancedAllocationArgs(nil, args); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\treturn &BalancedAllocation{\n\t\thandle: h,\n\t\tresourceAllocationScorer: resourceAllocationScorer{\n\t\t\tName:         BalancedAllocationName,\n\t\t\tscorer:       balancedResourceScorer,\n\t\t\tuseRequested: true,\n\t\t\tresources:    args.Resources,\n\t\t},\n\t}, nil\n}\n\nfunc balancedResourceScorer(requested, allocable []int64) int64 {\n\tvar resourceToFractions []float64\n\tvar totalFraction float64\n\tfor i := range requested {\n\t\tif allocable[i] == 0 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tfraction := float64(requested[i]) / float64(allocable[i])\n\t\tif fraction > 1 {\n\t\t\tfraction = 1\n\t\t}\n\t\ttotalFraction += fraction\n\t\tresourceToFractions = append(resourceToFractions, fraction)\n\t}\n\n\tstd := 0.0\n\n\t// For most cases, resources are limited to cpu and memory, the std could be simplified to std := (fraction1-fraction2)/2\n\t// len(fractions) > 2: calculate std based on the well-known formula - root square of Σ((fraction(i)-mean)^2)/len(fractions)\n\t// Otherwise, set the std to zero is enough.\n\tif len(resourceToFractions) == 2 {\n\t\tstd = math.Abs((resourceToFractions[0] - resourceToFractions[1]) / 2)\n\n\t} else if len(resourceToFractions) > 2 {\n\t\tmean := totalFraction / float64(len(resourceToFractions))\n\t\tvar sum float64\n\t\tfor _, fraction := range resourceToFractions {\n\t\t\tsum = sum + (fraction-mean)*(fraction-mean)\n\t\t}\n\t\tstd = math.Sqrt(sum / float64(len(resourceToFractions)))\n\t}\n\n\t// STD (standard deviation) is always a positive value. 1-deviation lets the score to be higher for node which has least deviation and\n\t// multiplying it with `MaxNodeScore` provides the scaling factor needed.\n\treturn int64((1 - std) * float64(framework.MaxNodeScore))\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2132,"cache_read_input_tokens":109526,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2132},"output_tokens":18,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01StGqzn8bNLSLrH43ofc9M9","session_id":"e9c52a39-b54c-4f29-a40c-65f97d21d7f0","uuid":"5eebbfee-8d6d-47bd-81eb-38daebf0ef7a"}

{\n\t// If a extender support preemption but have no cached node info, let's run filter to make sure\n\t// default scheduler's decision still stand with given pod and node.\n\tif !f.NodeCacheCapable and this node is not suitable for preemption.\n\tstatus := f.runPredicate(pod, nodeInfoCopy)\n\tif status.IsSuccess() {\n\t\t// pass\n\t} else if status.IsRejected() {\n\t\t// does not fit\n\t\treturn nil, 0, false, nil\n\t} else {\n\t\t// internal errors\n\t\treturn nil, 0, false, status.AsError()\n\t}\n\n\tvar victims []*v1.Pod\n\n\t// TODO(harry): handle PDBs in the future.\n\tnumViolatingVictim interface.\n414: func (f *frameworkImpl) Close() error {\n415: \tvar errs []error\n416: \tfor name, plugin := range f.pluginsMap {\n417: \t\tif closer, ok := plugin.(io.Closer); ok {\n418: \t\t\terr := closer.Close()\n419: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n420: \t\t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"%s failed to close: %w\", name, err))\n421: \t\t\t\t// We try to close all plugins even if we got errors from some.\n422: \t\t\t}\n423: \t\t}\n424: \t}\n425: \treturn errors.Join(errs...)\n426: }\n427: \n428: // getScoreWeights makes sure that, between MultiPoint-Score plugin weights and individual Score\n429: // plugin weights there is not an overflow of MaxTotalScore.\n430: func getScoreWeights(f *frameworkImpl, plugins []config.Plugin) error {\n431: \tvar totalPriority int64\n432: \tscorePlugins := reflect.ValueOf(&f.scorePlugins).Elem()\n433: \tpluginType := scorePlugins.Type().Elem()\n434: \tfor _, e := range plugins {\n435: \t\tpg := f.pluginsMap[e.Name]\n436: \t\tif !reflect.TypeOf(pg).Implements(pluginType) {\n437: \t\t\tcontinue\n438: \t\t}\n439: \n440: \t\t// We append MultiPoint plugins to the list of Score plugins. So if this plugin has already been\n441: \t\t// encountered, let the individual Score weight take precedence.\n442: \t\tif _, ok := f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name]; ok {\n443: \t\t\tcontinue\n444: \t\t}\n445: \t\t// a weight of zero is not permitted, plugins can be disabled explicitly\n446: \t\t// when configured.\n447: \t\tf.scorePluginWeight[e.Name] = int(e.Weight)\n448: \t\tif f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name] == 0 {\n449: \t\t\tf.scorePluginWeight[e.Name] = 1\n450: \t\t}\n451: \n452: \t\t// Checks totalPriority against MaxTotalScore to avoid overflow\n453: \t\tif int64(f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name])*framework.MaxNodeScore > framework.MaxTotalScore-totalPriority {\n454: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"total score of Score plugins could overflow\")\n455: \t\t}\n456: \t\ttotalPriority += int64(f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name]) * framework.MaxNodeScore\n457: \t}\n458: \treturn nil\n459: }\n460: \n461: type orderedSet struct {\n462: \tset         map[string]int\n463: \tlist        []string\n464: \tdeletionCnt int\n465: }\n466: \n467: func newOrderedSet() *orderedSet {\n468: \treturn &orderedSet{set: make(map[string]int)}\n469: }\n470: \n471: func (os *orderedSet) insert(s string) {\n472: \tif os.has(s) {\n473: \t\treturn\n474: \t}\n475: \tos.set[s] = len(os.list)\n476: \tos.list = append(os.list, s)\n477: }\n478: \n479: func (os *orderedSet) has(s string) bool {\n480: \t_, found := os.set[s]\n481: \treturn found\n482: }\n483: \n484: func (os *orderedSet) delete(s string) {\n485: \tif i, found := os.set[s]; found {\n486: \t\tdelete(os.set, s)\n487: \t\tos.list = append(os.list[:i-os.deletionCnt], os.list[i+1-os.deletionCnt:]...)\n488: \t\tos.deletionCnt++\n489: \t}\n490: }\n491: \n492: func (f *frameworkImpl) expandMultiPointPlugins(logger klog.Logger, profile *config.KubeSchedulerProfile) error {\n493: \t// initialize MultiPoint plugins\n494: \tfor _, e := range f.getExtensionPoints(profile.Plugins) {\n495: \t\tplugins := reflect.ValueOf(e.slicePtr).Elem()\n496: \t\tpluginType := plugins.Type().Elem()\n497: \t\t// build enabledSet of plugins already registered via normal extension points\n498: \t\t// to check double registration\n499: \t\tenabledSet := newOrderedSet()\n500: \t\tfor _, plugin := range e.plugins.Enabled {\n501: \t\t\tenabledSet.insert(plugin.Name)\n502: \t\t}\n503: \n504: \t\tdisabledSet := sets.New[string]()\n505: \t\tfor _, disabledPlugin := range e.plugins.Disabled {\n506: \t\t\tdisabledSet.Insert(disabledPlugin.Name)\n507: \t\t}\n508: \t\tif disabledSet.Has(\"*\") {\n509: \t\t\tlogger.V(4).Info(\"Skipped MultiPoint expansion because all plugins are disabled for extension point\", \"extension\", pluginType)\n510: \t\t\tcontinue\n511: \t\t}\n512: \n513: \t\t// track plugins enabled via multipoint separately from those enabled by specific extensions,\n514: \t\t// so that we can distinguish between double-registration and explicit overrides\n515: \t\tmultiPointEnabled := newOrderedSet()\n516: \t\toverridePlugins := newOrderedSet()\n517: \t\tfor _, ep := range profile.Plugins.MultiPoint.Enabled {\n518: \t\t\tpg, ok := f.pluginsMap[ep.Name]\n519: \t\t\tif !ok {\n520: \t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"%s %q does not exist\", pluginType.Name(), ep.Name)\n521: \t\t\t}\n522: \n523: \t\t\t// if this plugin doesn't implement the type for the current extension we're trying to expand, skip\n524: \t\t\tif !reflect.TypeOf(pg).Implements(pluginType) {\n525: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n526: \t\t\t}\n527: \n528: \t\t\t// a plugin that's enabled via MultiPoint can still be disabled for specific extension points\n529: \t\t\tif disabledSet.Has(ep.Name) {\n530: \t\t\t\tlogger.V(4).Info(\"Skipped disabled plugin for extension point\", \"plugin\", ep.Name, \"extension\", pluginType)\n531: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n532: \t\t\t}\n533: \n534: \t\t\t// if this plugin has already been enabled by the specific extension point,\n535: \t\t\t// the user intent is to override the default plugin or make some other explicit setting.\n536: \t\t\t// Either way, discard the MultiPoint value for this plugin.\n537: \t\t\t// This maintains expected behavior for overriding default plugins (see https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/99582)\n538: \t\t\tif enabledSet.has(ep.Name) {\n539: \t\t\t\toverridePlugins.insert(ep.Name)\n540: \t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"MultiPoint plugin is explicitly re-configured; overriding\", \"plugin\", ep.Name)\n541: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n542: \t\t\t}\n543: \n544: \t\t\t// if this plugin is already registered via MultiPoint, then this is\n545: \t\t\t// a double registration and an error in the config.\n546: \t\t\tif multiPointEnabled.has(ep.Name) {\n547: \t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"plugin %q already registered as %q\", ep.Name, pluginType.Name())\n548: \t\t\t}\n549: \n550: \t\t\t// we only need to update the multipoint set, since we already have the specific extension set from above\n551: \t\t\tmultiPointEnabled.insert(ep.Name)\n552: \t\t}\n553: \n554: \t\t// Reorder plugins. Here is the expected order:\n555: \t\t// - part 1: overridePlugins. Their order stay intact as how they're specified in regular extension point.\n556: \t\t// - part 2: multiPointEnabled - i.e., plugin defined in multipoint but not in regular extension point.\n557: \t\t// - part 3: other plugins (excluded by part 1 & 2) in regular extension point.\n558: \t\tnewPlugins := reflect.New(reflect.TypeOf(e.slicePtr).Elem()).Elem()\n559: \t\t// part 1\n560: \t\tfor _, name := range slice.CopyStrings(enabledSet.list) {\n561: \t\t\tif overridePlugins.has(name) {\n562: \t\t\t\tnewPlugins = reflect.Append(newPlugins, reflect.ValueOf(f.pluginsMap[name]))\n563: \t\t\t\tenabledSet.delete(name)\n564: \t\t\t}\n565: \t\t}\n566: \t\t// part 2\n567: \t\tfor _, name := range multiPointEnabled.list {\n568: \t\t\tnewPlugins = reflect.Append(newPlugins, reflect.ValueOf(f.pluginsMap[name]))\n569: \t\t}\n570: \t\t// part 3\n571: \t\tfor _, name := range enabledSet.list {\n572: \t\t\tnewPlugins = reflect.Append(newPlugins, reflect.ValueOf(f.pluginsMap[name]))\n573: \t\t}\n574: \t\tplugins.Set(newPlugins)\n575: \t}\n576: \treturn nil\n577: }\n578: \n579: func shouldHaveEnqueueExtensions(p framework.Plugin) bool {\n580: \tswitch p.(type) {\n581: \t// Only PreEnqueue, PreFilter, Filter, Reserve, and Permit plugins can (should) have EnqueueExtensions.\n582: \t// See the comment of EnqueueExtensions for more detailed reason here.\n583: \tcase framework.PreEnqueuePlugin, framework.PreFilterPlugin, framework.FilterPlugin, framework.ReservePlugin, framework.PermitPlugin:\n584: \t\treturn true\n585: \t}\n586: \treturn false\n587: }\n588: \n589: func (f *frameworkImpl) fillEnqueueExtensions(p framework.Plugin) {\n590: \tif !shouldHaveEnqueueExtensions(p) {\n591: \t\t// Ignore EnqueueExtensions from plugin which isn't PreEnqueue, PreFilter, Filter, Reserve, and Permit.\n592: \t\treturn\n593: \t}\n594: \n595: \text, ok := p.(framework.EnqueueExtensions)\n596: \tif !ok {\n597: \t\t// If interface backward compatibility.\n600: \t\tf.enqueueExtensions = append(f.enqueueExtensions, &defaultEnqueueExtension{pluginName: p.Name()})\n601: \t\treturn\n602: \t}\n603: \n604: \tf.enqueueExtensions = append(f.enqueueExtensions, ext)\n605: }\n606: \n607: // defaultEnqueueExtension is used when a plugin does not implement EnqueueExtensions interface.\n608: type defaultEnqueueExtension struct {\n609: \tpluginName string\n610: }\n611: \n612: func (p *defaultEnqueueExtension) Name() string { return p.pluginName }\n613: func (p *defaultEnqueueExtension) EventsToRegister() []framework.ClusterEventWithHint {\n614: \t// need to return all specific cluster events with framework.All action instead of wildcard event\n615: \t// because the returning values are used to register event handlers.\n616: \t// If we return the wildcard here, it won't affect the event handlers registered by the plugin\n617: \t// and some events may not be registered in the event handlers.\n618: \treturn framework.UnrollWildCardResource()\n619: }\n620: \n621: func updatePluginList(pluginList interface{}, pluginSet config.PluginSet, pluginsMap map[string]framework.Plugin) error {\n622: \tplugins := reflect.ValueOf(pluginList).Elem()\n623: \tpluginType := plugins.Type().Elem()\n624: \tset := sets.New[string]()\n625: \tfor _, ep := range pluginSet.Enabled {\n626: \t\tpg, ok := {\n632: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"plugin %q does not extend %s plugin\", ep.Name, pluginType.Name())\n633: \t\t}\n634: \n635: \t\tif set.Has(ep.Name) {\n636: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"plugin %q QueueSortFunc() framework.LessFunc {\n659: \tif f == nil {\n660: \t\t// If frameworkImpl is nil, simply keep their order unchanged.\n661: \t\t// NOTE: this is primarily for tests.\n662: \t\treturn It returns\n674: // *Status and its code is set to non-success if any of the plugins returns\n675: // anything but Success/Skip.\n676: // When it returns Skip status, returned PreFilterResult and other fields in status are just ignored,\n677: // and coupled Filter plugin/PreFilterExtensions() will be skipped in this scheduling cycle.\n678: // If a non-success status is returned, then the scheduling cycle is aborted.\n679: func (f *frameworkImpl) RunPreFilterPlugins(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (_ *framework.PreFilterResult, status *framework.Status) {\n680: \tstartTime := time.Now()\n681: \tskipPlugins := sets.New[string]()\n682: \tdefer func() {\n683: \t\tstate.SkipFilterPlugins = skipPlugins\n684: \t\tmetrics.FrameworkExtensionPointDuration.WithLabelValues(metrics.PreFilter, status.Code().String(), f.profileName).Observe(metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\n685: \t}()\n686: \tvar result *framework.PreFilterResult\n687: \tvar pluginsWithNodes []string\n688: \tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n689: \tverboseLogs := logger.V(4).Enabled()\n690: \tif verboseLogs {\n691: \t\tlogger = klog.LoggerWithName(logger, \"PreFilter\")\n692: \t}\n693: \tvar returnStatus *framework.Status\n694: \tfor _, pl := range f.preFilterPlugins {\n695: \t\tctx := ctx\n696: \t\tif verboseLogs {\n697: \t\t\tlogger := klog.LoggerWithName(logger, pl.Name())\n698: \t\t\tctx = klog.NewContext(ctx, logger)\n699: \t\t}\n700: \t\tr, s := f.runPreFilterPlugin(ctx, pl, state, pod)\n701: \t\tif s.IsSkip() {\n702: \t\t\tskipPlugins.Insert(pl.Name())\n703: \t\t\tcontinue\n704: \t\t}\n705: \t\tif !s.IsSuccess() {\n706: \t\t\ts.SetPlugin(pl.Name())\n707: \t\t\tif s.Code() == framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable {\n708: \t\t\t\t// In this case, the preemption shouldn't happen in this scheduling cycle.\n709: \t\t\t\t// So, no need to execute all PreFilter.\n710: \t\t\t\treturn nil, s\n711: \t\t\t}\n712: \t\t\tif s.Code() == framework.Unschedulable {\n713: \t\t\t\t// In this case, the preemption should happen later in this scheduling cycle.\n714: \t\t\t\t// So we need to execute all PreFilter.\n715: \t\t\t\t// https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/119770\n716: \t\t\t\treturnStatus = s\n717: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n718: \t\t\t}\n719: \t\t\treturn nil, framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"running PreFilter plugin %q: %w\", pl.Name(), s.AsError())).WithPlugin(pl.Name())\n720: \t\t}\n721: \t\tif !r.AllNodes() {\n722: \t\t\tpluginsWithNodes = append(pluginsWithNodes, pl.Name())\n723: \t\t}\n724: \t\tresult = result.Merge(r)\n725: \t\tif !result.AllNodes() && len(result.NodeNames) == 0 {\n726: \t\t\tmsg := fmt.Sprintf(\"node(s) didn't satisfy plugin(s) %v simultaneously\", pluginsWithNodes)\n727: \t\t\tif len(pluginsWithNodes) == 1 {\n728: \t\t\t\tmsg = fmt.Sprintf(\"node(s) didn't satisfy plugin %v\", pluginsWithNodes[0])\n729: \t\t\t}\n730: \n731: \t\t\t// When PreFilterResult filters out Nodes, the framework considers Nodes that are filtered out as getting \"UnschedulableAndUnresolvable\".\n732: \t\t\treturn result, framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, msg)\n733: \t\t}\n734: \t}\n735: \treturn result, returnStatus\n736: }\n737: \n738: func (f *frameworkImpl) runPreFilterPlugin(ctx context.Context, pl framework.PreFilterPlugin, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (*framework.PreFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\n739: \tif !state.ShouldRecordPluginMetrics() {\n740: \tf.metricsRecorder.ObservePluginDurationAsync(metrics.PreFilter, pl.Name(), status.Code().String(), metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\n745: \treturn result, status\n746: }\n747: \n748: // (f *frameworkImpl) RunFilterPlugins(\n843: \tctx context.Context,\n844: \tstate *framework.CycleState,\n845: \tpod *v1.Pod,\n846: \tnodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo,\n847: ) *framework.Status {\n848: execute all plugins.\n889: func (f *frameworkImpl) RunPostFilterPlugins(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, filteredNodeStatusMap framework.NodeToStatusMap) (_ *framework.PostFilterResult, status *framework.Status) {\n890: \tstartTime := time.Now()\n891: \tdefer func() {\n892: \t\tmetrics.FrameworkExtensionPointDuration.WithLabelValues(metrics.PostFilter, status.Code().String(), f.profileName).Observe(metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\n893: \t}()\n894: \n895: \tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n896: \tverboseLogs := logger.V(4).Enabled()\n897: \tif verboseLogs {\n898: \t\tlogger = klog.LoggerWithName(logger, \"PostFilter\")\n899: \t}\n900: \n901: \t// `result` records the last meaningful(non-noop) PostFilterResult.\n902: \tvar result *framework.PostFilterResult\n903: \tvar reasons []string\n904: \tvar rejectorPlugin string\n905: \tfor _, pl := range f.postFilterPlugins {\n906: \t\tctx := ctx\n907: \t\tif verboseLogs {\n908: \t\t\tlogger := klog.LoggerWithName(logger, pl.Name())\n909: \t\t\tctx = klog.NewContext(ctx, logger)\n910: \t\t}\n911: \t\tr, s := f.runPostFilterPlugin(ctx, pl, state, pod, filteredNodeStatusMap)\n912: \t\tif s.IsSuccess() {\n913: \t\t\treturn r, s\n914: \t\t} else if s.Code() == framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable {\n915: \t\t\treturn r, s.WithPlugin(pl.Name())\n916: \t\t} else if !s.IsRejected() {\n917: \t\t\t// Any status other than Success, Unschedulable or UnschedulableAndUnresolvable is Error.\n918: \t\t\treturn nil, framework.AsStatus(s.AsError()).WithPlugin(pl.Name())\n919: \t\t} else if r != nil && r.Mode() != framework.ModeNoop {\n920: \t\t\tresult = r\n921: \t\t}\n922: \n923: \t\treasons = append(reasons, s.Reasons()...)\n924: \t\t// Record the first failed plugin unless we proved that\n925: \t\t// the latter is more relevant.\n926: \t\tif len(rejectorPlugin) == 0 {\n927: \t\t\trejectorPlugin = pl.Name()\n928: \t\t}\n929: \t}\n930: \n931: \treturn result, framework.NewStatus(framework.Unschedulable, reasons...).WithPlugin(rejectorPlugin)\n932: }\n933: \n934: func (f *frameworkImpl) runPostFilterPlugin(ctx context.Context, pl framework.PostFilterPlugin, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, filteredNodeStatusMap framework.NodeToStatusMap) (*framework.PostFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\n935: \tif !state.ShouldRecordPluginMetrics() {\n936: \t\treturn pl.PostFilter(ctx, state, pod, filteredNodeStatusMap)\n937: \t}\n938: \tstartTime := time.Now()\n939: \tr, s := pl.PostFilter(ctx, state, pod, filteredNodeStatusMap)\n940: \tf.metricsRecorder.ObservePluginDurationAsync(metrics.PostFilter, pl.Name(), s.Code().String(), metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\n941: \treturn r, s\n942: }\n943: \n944: // RunFilterPluginsWithNominatedPods runs the set of configured filter plugins\n945: // for nominated pod on the given node.\n946: // This function is called from two different places: Schedule and Preempt.\n947: // When it is called from Schedule, we want to test whether the pod is\n948: // schedulable on the node with all the existing pods on the node plus higher\n949: // and equal priority pods nominated to run on the node.\n950: // When it is called from Preempt, we should remove the victims of preemption\n951: // and add the nominated pods. Removal of the victims is done by\n952: // SelectVictimsOnNode(). Preempt removes victims from PreFilter state and\n953: // NodeInfo before calling this function.\n954: func (f *frameworkImpl) RunFilterPluginsWithNominatedPods(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, info *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n955: \tvar status *framework.Status\n956: \n957: \tpodsAdded := false\n958: \t// We run filters twice in some cases. If the node has greater or equal priority\n959: \t// nominated pods, we run them when those pods are added to PreFilter state and nodeInfo.\n960: \t// If all filters succeed in this pass, we run them again when these\n961: \t// nominated pods are not added. This second pass is necessary because some\n962: \t// filters such as inter-pod affinity may not pass without the nominated pods.\n963: \t// If there are no nominated pods for the node or if the first run of the\n964: \t// filters fail, we don't run the second pass.\n965: \t// We consider only equal or higher priority pods in the first pass, because\n966: \t// those are the current \"pod\" must yield to them and not take a space opened\n967: \t// for running them. It is ok if the current \"pod\" take resources freed for\n968: \t// lower priority pods.\n969: \t// Requiring that the new pod is schedulable in both circumstances ensures that\n970: \t// we are making a conservative decision: filters like resources and inter-pod\n971: \t// anti-affinity are more likely to fail when the nominated pods are treated\n972: \t// as running, while filters like pod affinity are more likely to fail when\n973: \t// the nominated pods are treated as not running. We can't just assume the\n974: \t// nominated pods are running because they are not running right now and in fact,\n975: \t// they may end up getting scheduled to a different node.\n976: \tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n977: \tlogger = klog.LoggerWithName(logger, \"FilterWithNominatedPods\")\n978: \tctx = klog.NewContext(ctx, logger)\n979: \tfor i := 0; i < 2; i++ {\n980: \t\tstateToUse := state\n981: \t\tnodeInfoToUse := info\n982: \t\tif i == 0 {\n983: \t\t\tvar err error\n984: \t\t\tpodsAdded, stateToUse, nodeInfoToUse, err = addNominatedPods(ctx, f, pod, state, info)\n985: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n986: \t\t\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n987: \t\t\t}\n988: \t\t} else if !podsAdded || !status.IsSuccess() {\n989: \t\t\tbreak\n990: \t\t}\n991: \n992: \t\tstatus = f.RunFilterPlugins(ctx, stateToUse, pod, nodeInfoToUse)\n993: \t\tif !status.IsSuccess() && !status.IsRejected() {\n994: \t\t\treturn status\n995: \t\t}\n996: \t}\n997: \n998: \treturn status\n999: }\n1000: \n1001: // addNominatedPods adds pods with equal or greater priority which are nominated\n1002: // to run on the node. It returns 1) whether any pod was added, 2) augmented cycleState,\n1003: // 3) augmented nodeInfo.\n1004: func addNominatedPods(ctx context.Context, fh framework.Handle, pod *v1.Pod, state *framework.CycleState, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) (bool, *framework.CycleState, *framework.NodeInfo, error) {\n1005: \tif fh == nil {\n1006: \t\t// This may happen only in tests.\n1007: \t\treturn false, state, nodeInfo, nil\n1008: \t}\n1009: \tnominatedPodInfos := fh.NominatedPodsForNode(nodeInfo.Node().Name)\n1010: \tif len(nominatedPodInfos) == 0 {\n1011: \t\treturn false, state, nodeInfo, nil\n1012: \t}\n1013: \tnodeInfoOut := nodeInfo.Snapshot()\n1014: \tstateOut := state.Clone()\n1015: \tpodsAdded := false\n1016: \tfor _, pi := range nominatedPodInfos {\n1017: \t\tif corev1.PodPriority(pi.Pod) >= corev1.PodPriority(pod) && pi.Pod.UID != pod.UID {\n1018: \t\t\tnodeInfoOut.AddPodInfo(pi)\n1019: \t\t\tstatus := fh.RunPreFilterExtensionAddPod(ctx, stateOut, pod, pi, nodeInfoOut)\n1020: \t\t\tif !status.IsSuccess() {\n1021: \t\t\t\treturn false, state, nodeInfo, status.AsError()\n1022: \t\t\t}\n1023: \t\t\tpodsAdded = true\n1024: \t\t}\n1025: \t}\n1026: \treturn podsAdded, stateOut, nodeInfoOut, nil\n1027: }\n1028: \n1029: // RunPreScorePlugins runs the set of configured pre-score plugins. If any\n1030: // of these plugins returns any status other than Success/Skip, the given pod is rejected.\n1031: // When it returns Skip status, other fields in status are just ignored,\n1032: // and coupled Score plugin will be skipped in this scheduling cycle.\n1033: func (f *frameworkImpl) RunPreScorePlugins(\n1034: \tctx context.Context,\n1035: \tstate *framework.CycleState,\n1036: \tpod *v1.Pod,\n1037: \tnodes []*framework.NodeInfo,\n1038: ) (status *framework.Status) {\n1039: \tstartTime := time.Now()\n1040: \tskipPlugins := sets.New[string]()\n1041: \tdefer func() {\n1042: \t\tstate.SkipScorePlugins = skipPlugins\n1043: \t\tmetrics.FrameworkExtensionPointDuration.WithLabelValues(metrics.PreScore, status.Code().String(), f.profileName).Observe(metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\n1044: \t}()\n1045: \tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n1046: \tverboseLogs := logger.V(4).Enabled()\n1047: \tif verboseLogs {\n1048: \t\tlogger = klog.LoggerWithName(logger, \"PreScore\")\n1049: \t}\n1050: \tfor _, pl := range f.preScorePlugins {\n1051: \t\tctx := ctx\n1052: \t\tif verboseLogs {\n1053: \t\t\tlogger := klog.LoggerWithName(logger, pl.Name())\n1054: \t\t\tctx = klog.NewContext(ctx, logger)\n1055: \t\t}\n1056: \t\tstatus = f.runPreScorePlugin(ctx, pl, state, pod, nodes)\n1057: \t\tif status.IsSkip() %w\", pl.Name(), status.AsError()))\n1063: \t\t}\n1064: \t}\n1065: \treturn nil\n1066: }\n1067: \n1068: func (f *frameworkImpl) runPreScorePlugin(ctx context.Context, pl framework.PreScorePlugin, \t}\n1072: \tstartTime := time.Now()\n1073: \tstatus := pl.PreScore(ctx, state, pod, nodes)\n1074: \tf.metricsRecorder.ObservePluginDurationAsync(metrics.PreScore, pl.Name(), status.Code().String(), \\t\\tos.deletionCnt++\\n489: \\t}\\n490: }\\n491: \\n492: func (f *frameworkImpl) expandMultiPointPlugins(logger klog.Logger, profile *config.KubeSchedulerProfile) error {\\n493: \\t// initialize ends up being unschedulable,\\n273: \\t\\t// we put framework.ExtenderName to pInfo.UnschedulablePlugins.\\n274: \\t\\tf.enqueueExtensions = []framework.EnqueueExtensions{&defaultEnqueueExtension{pluginName: framework.ExtenderName}}\\n275: \\t}\\n276: \\n277: \\tif profile == nil {\\n278: \\t\\treturn f, nil\\n279: \\t}\\n280: \\n281: \\tf.profileName = profile.SchedulerName\\n282: \\tf.percentageOfNodesToScore = profile.PercentageOfNodesToScore\\n283: \\tif profile.Plugins == nil {\\n284: \\t\\treturn f, nil\\n285: \\t}\\n286:      f.profileName,\\n300: \\t\\tPercentageOfNodesToScore: f.percentageOfNodesToScore,\\n301: \\t\\tPlugins:                  profile.Plugins,\\n302: \\t\\tPluginConfig:             make([]config.PluginConfig, 0, len(pg)),\\n303: \\t}\\n304: \\n305: \\tf.pluginsMap = make(map[string]framework.Plugin)\\n306: \\tfor name, factory := range r {\\n307: \\t\\t// initialize only needed \\n335: \\t// initialize multiPoint plugins to their expanded extension points\\n336: \\tif len(profile.Plugins.MultiPoint.Enabled) > 0 {\\n337: \\t\\tif err := f.expandMultiPointPlugins(logger, }\\n377: \\n378: // setInstrumentedPlugins initializes instrumented plugins from current plugins that frameworkImpl has.\\n379: func (f *frameworkImpl) setInstrumentedPlugins() {\\n380: \\t// Cache getScoreWeights makes sure that, between MultiPoint-Score plugin weights and individual Score\\n429: // plugin weights there is not an overflow of MaxTotalScore.\\n430: func getScoreWeights(f *frameworkImpl, plugins []config.Plugin) error {\\n431: \\tvar totalPriority int64\\n432: \\tscorePlugins := reflect.ValueOf(&f.scorePlugins).Elem()\\n433: \\tpluginType := scorePlugins.Type().Elem()\\n434: \\tfor _, e := range plugins {\\n435: \\t\\tpg := f.pluginsMap[e.Name]\\n436: \\t\\tif !reflect.TypeOf(pg).Implements(pluginType) {\\n437: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n438: \\t\\t}\\n439: \\n440: \\t\\t// We append MultiPoint plugins to the list of Score plugins. So if this plugin has already been\\n441: \\t\\t// encountered, let the individual Score weight take precedence.\\n442: \\t\\tif _, ok := f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name]; ok {\\n443: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n444: \\t\\t}\\n445: \\t\\t// a weight of zero is not permitted, plugins can be disabled explicitly\\n446: \\t\\t// when configured.\\n447: \\t\\tf.scorePluginWeight[e.Name] = int(e.Weight)\\n448: \\t\\tif f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name] == 0 {\\n449: \\t\\t\\tf.scorePluginWeight[e.Name] = 1\\n450: \\t\\t}\\n451: \\n452: \\t\\t// Checks totalPriority against MaxTotalScore to avoid overflow\\n453: \\t\\tif int64(f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name])*framework.MaxNodeScore > framework.MaxTotalScore-totalPriority {\\n454: \\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"total score of Score plugins could overflow\\\")\\n455: \\t\\t}\\n456: \\t\\ttotalPriority += int64(f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name]) * framework.MaxNodeScore\\n457: \\t}\\n458: \\treturn nil\\n459: }\\n460: \\n461: type orderedSet struct {\\n462: \\tset         map[string]int\\n463: \\tlist        []string\\n464: \\tdeletionCnt int\\n465: }\\n466: \\n467: func newOrderedSet() *orderedSet {\\n468: \\treturn &orderedSet{set: make(map[string]int)}\\n469: }\\n470: \\n471: func (os *orderedSet) insert(s string) {\\n472: \\tif os.has(s) {\\n473: \\t\\treturn\\n474: \\t}\\n475: \\tos.set[s] = len(os.list)\\n476: \\tos.list = append(os.list, s)\\n477: }\\n478: \\n479: func (os *orderedSet) has(s string) bool {\\n480: \\t_, found := os.set[s]\\n481: \\treturn found\\n482: }\\n483: \\n484: func (os *orderedSet) delete(s string) {\\n485: \\tif i, found := os.set[s]; found {\\n486: \\t\\tdelete(os.set, s)\\n487: \\t\\tos.list = append(os.list[:i-os.deletionCnt], os.list[i+1-os.deletionCnt:]...)\\n488: \\t\\tos.deletionCnt++\\n489: \\t}\\n490: }\\n491: \\n492: func (f *frameworkImpl) expandMultiPointPlugins(logger klog.Logger, profile *config.KubeSchedulerProfile) error {\\n493: \\t// initialize MultiPoint plugins\\n494: \\tfor _, e := range f.getExtensionPoints(profile.Plugins) {\\n495: \\t\\tplugins := reflect.ValueOf(e.slicePtr).Elem()\\n496: \\t\\tpluginType := plugins.Type().Elem()\\n497: \\t\\t// build enabledSet of plugins already registered via normal extension points\\n498: \\t\\t// to check double registration\\n499: \\t\\tenabledSet := newOrderedSet()\\n500: \\t\\tfor _, plugin := range e.plugins.Enabled {\\n501: \\t\\t\\tenabledSet.insert(plugin.Name)\\n502: \\t\\t}\\n503: \\n504: \\t\\tdisabledSet := sets.New[string]()\\n505: \\t\\tfor _, disabledPlugin := range e.plugins.Disabled {\\n506: \\t\\t\\tdisabledSet.Insert(disabledPlugin.Name)\\n507: \\t\\t}\\n508: \\t\\tif disabledSet.Has(\\\"*\\\") {\\n509: \\t\\t\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"Skipped MultiPoint expansion because all plugins are disabled for extension point\\\", \\\"extension\\\", pluginType)\\n510: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n511: \\t\\t}\\n512: \\n513: \\t\\t// track plugins enabled via multipoint separately from those enabled by specific extensions,\\n514: \\t\\t// so that we can distinguish between double-registration and explicit overrides\\n515: \\t\\tmultiPointEnabled := newOrderedSet()\\n516: \\t\\toverridePlugins := newOrderedSet()\\n517: \\t\\tfor _, ep := range profile.Plugins.MultiPoint.Enabled {\\n518: \\t\\t\\tpg, ok := f.pluginsMap[ep.Name]\\n519: \\t\\t\\tif !ok {\\n520: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"%s %q does not exist\\\", pluginType.Name(), ep.Name)\\n521: \\t\\t\\t}\\n522: \\n523: \\t\\t\\t// if this plugin doesn't implement the type for the current extension we're trying to expand, skip\\n524: \\t\\t\\tif !reflect.TypeOf(pg).Implements(pluginType) {\\n525: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n526: \\t\\t\\t}\\n527: \\n528: \\t\\t\\t// a plugin that's enabled via MultiPoint can still be disabled for specific extension points\\n529: \\t\\t\\tif disabledSet.Has(ep.Name) {\\n530: \\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"Skipped disabled plugin for extension point\\\", \\\"plugin\\\", ep.Name, \\\"extension\\\", pluginType)\\n531: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n532: \\t\\t\\t}\\n533: \\n534: \\t\\t\\t// if this plugin has already been enabled by the specific extension point,\\n535: \\t\\t\\t// the user intent is to override the default plugin or make some other explicit setting.\\n536: \\t\\t\\t// Either way, discard the MultiPoint value for this plugin.\\n537: \\t\\t\\t// This maintains expected behavior for overriding default plugins (see https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/99582)\\n538: \\t\\t\\tif enabledSet.has(ep.Name) {\\n539: \\t\\t\\t\\toverridePlugins.insert(ep.Name)\\n540: \\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"MultiPoint plugin is explicitly re-configured; overriding\\\", \\\"plugin\\\", ep.Name)\\n541: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n542: \\t\\t\\t}\\n543: \\n544: \\t\\t\\t// if this plugin is already registered via MultiPoint, then this is\\n545: \\t\\t\\t// a double registration and an error in the config.\\n546: \\t\\t\\tif multiPointEnabled.has(ep.Name) {\\n547: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"plugin %q already registered as %q\\\", ep.Name, pluginType.Name())\\n548: \\t\\t\\t}\\n549: \\n550: \\t\\t\\t// we only need to update the multipoint set, since we already have the specific extension set from above\\n551: \\t\\t\\tmultiPointEnabled.insert(ep.Name)\\n552: \\t\\t}\\n553: \\n554: \\t\\t// Reorder plugins. Here is the expected order:\\n555: \\t\\t// - part 1: overridePlugins. Their order stay intact as how they're specified in regular extension point.\\n556: \\t\\t// - part 2: multiPointEnabled - i.e., plugin defined in multipoint but not in regular extension point.\\n557: \\t\\t// - part 3: other plugins (excluded by part 1 & 2) in regular extension point.\\n558: \\t\\tnewPlugins := reflect.New(reflect.TypeOf(e.slicePtr).Elem()).Elem()\\n559: \\t\\t// part 1\\n560: \\t\\t\\tnewPlugins = reflect.Append(newPlugins, reflect.ValueOf(f.pluginsMap[name]))\\n573: \\t\\t}\\n574: \\t\\tplugins.Set(newPlugins)\\n575: \\t}\\n576: \\treturn nil\\n577: }\\n578: \\n579: func getScoreWeights makes sure that, between MultiPoint-Score plugin weights and individual Score\\n429: // plugin weights there is not an overflow of MaxTotalScore.\\n430: func getScoreWeights(f *frameworkImpl, plugins []config.Plugin) error {\\n431: \\tvar totalPriority int64\\n432: \\tscorePlugins := reflect.ValueOf(&f.scorePlugins).Elem()\\n433: \\tpluginType := scorePlugins.Type().Elem()\\n434: \\tfor _, e := range plugins {\\n435: \\t\\tpg := f.pluginsMap[e.Name]\\n436: \\t\\tif !reflect.TypeOf(pg).Implements(pluginType) {\\n437: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n438: \\t\\t}\\n439: \\n440: \\t\\t// We append MultiPoint plugins to the list of Score plugins. So if this plugin has already been\\n441: \\t\\t// encountered, let the individual Score weight take precedence.\\n442: \\t\\tif _, ok := f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name]; ok {\\n443: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n444: \\t\\t}\\n445: \\t\\t// a weight of zero is not permitted, plugins can be disabled explicitly\\n446: \\t\\t// when configured.\\n447: \\t\\tf.scorePluginWeight[e.Name] = int(e.Weight)\\n448: \\t\\tif f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name] == 0 {\\n449: \\t\\t\\tf.scorePluginWeight[e.Name] = 1\\n450: \\t\\t}\\n451: \\n452: \\t\\t// Checks totalPriority against MaxTotalScore to avoid overflow\\n453: \\t\\tif int64(f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name])*framework.MaxNodeScore > framework.MaxTotalScore-totalPriority {\\n454: \\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"total score of Score plugins could overflow\\\")\\n455: \\t\\t}\\n456: \\t\\ttotalPriority += int64(f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name]) * framework.MaxNodeScore\\n457: \\t}\\n458: \\treturn nil\\n459: }\\n460: \\n461: type orderedSet struct {\\n462: \\tset         map[string]int\\n463: \\tlist        []string\\n464: \\tdeletionCnt int\\n465: }\\n466: \\n467: func newOrderedSet() *orderedSet {\\n468: \\treturn &orderedSet{set: make(map[string]int)}\\n469: }\\n470: \\n471: func (os *orderedSet) insert(s string) {\\n472: \\tif os.has(s) {\\n473: \\t\\treturn\\n474: \\t}\\n475: \\tos.set[s] = len(os.list)\\n476: \\tos.list = append(os.list, s)\\n477: }\\n478: \\n479: func (os *orderedSet) has(s string) bool {\\n480: \\t_, found := os.set[s]\\n481: \\treturn found\\n482: }\\n483: \\n484: func (os *orderedSet) delete(s string) {\\n485: \\tif i, found := os.set[s]; found {\\n486: \\t\\tdelete(os.set, s)\\n487: \\t\\tos.list = append(os.list[:i-os.deletionCnt], os.list[i+1-os.deletionCnt:]...)\\n488: \\t\\tos.deletionCnt++\\n489: \\t}\\n490: }\\n491: \\n492: func (f *frameworkImpl) expandMultiPointPlugins(logger klog.Logger, profile *config.KubeSchedulerProfile) error {\\n493: \\t// initialize MultiPoint plugins\\n494: \\tfor _, e := range f.getExtensionPoints(profile.Plugins) {\\n495: \\t\\tplugins := reflect.ValueOf(e.slicePtr).Elem()\\n496: \\t\\tpluginType := plugins.Type().Elem()\\n497: \\t\\t// build enabledSet of plugins already registered via normal extension points\\n498: \\t\\t// to check double registration\\n499: \\t\\tenabledSet := newOrderedSet()\\n500: \\t\\tfor _, plugin := range e.plugins.Enabled {\\n501: \\t\\t\\tenabledSet.insert(plugin.Name)\\n502: \\t\\t}\\n503: \\n504: \\t\\tdisabledSet := sets.New[string]()\\n505: \\t\\tfor _, disabledPlugin := range e.plugins.Disabled {\\n506: \\t\\t\\tdisabledSet.Insert(disabledPlugin.Name)\\n507: \\t\\t}\\n508: \\t\\tif disabledSet.Has(\\\"*\\\") {\\n509: \\t\\t\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"Skipped MultiPoint expansion because all plugins are disabled for extension point\\\", \\\"extension\\\", pluginType)\\n510: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n511: \\t\\t}\\n512: \\n513: \\t\\t// track plugins enabled via multipoint separately from those enabled by specific extensions,\\n514: \\t\\t// so that we can distinguish between double-registration and explicit overrides\\n515: \\t\\tmultiPointEnabled := newOrderedSet()\\n516: \\t\\toverridePlugins := newOrderedSet()\\n517: \\t\\tfor _, ep := range profile.Plugins.MultiPoint.Enabled {\\n518: \\t\\t\\tpg, ok := f.pluginsMap[ep.Name]\\n519: \\t\\t\\tif !ok {\\n520: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"%s %q does not exist\\\", pluginType.Name(), ep.Name)\\n521: \\t\\t\\t}\\n522: \\n523: \\t\\t\\t// if this plugin doesn't implement the type for the current extension we're trying to expand, skip\\n524: \\t\\t\\tif !reflect.TypeOf(pg).Implements(pluginType) {\\n525: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n526: \\t\\t\\t}\\n527: \\n528: \\t\\t\\t// a plugin that's enabled via MultiPoint can still be disabled for specific extension points\\n529: \\t\\t\\tif disabledSet.Has(ep.Name) {\\n530: \\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"Skipped disabled plugin for extension point\\\", \\\"plugin\\\", ep.Name, \\\"extension\\\", pluginType)\\n531: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n532: \\t\\t\\t}\\n533: \\n534: \\t\\t\\t// if this plugin has already been enabled by the specific extension point,\\n535: \\t\\t\\t// the user intent is to override the default plugin or make some other explicit setting.\\n536: \\t\\t\\t// Either way, discard the MultiPoint value for this plugin.\\n537: \\t\\t\\t// This maintains expected behavior for overriding default plugins (see https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/99582)\\n538: \\t\\t\\tif enabledSet.has(ep.Name) {\\n539: \\t\\t\\t\\toverridePlugins.insert(ep.Name)\\n540: \\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"MultiPoint plugin is explicitly re-configured; overriding\\\", \\\"plugin\\\", ep.Name)\\n541: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n542: \\t\\t\\t}\\n543: \\n544: \\t\\t\\t// if this plugin is already registered via MultiPoint, then this is\\n545: \\t\\t\\t// a double registration and an error in the config.\\n546: \\t\\t\\tif multiPointEnabled.has(ep.Name) {\\n547: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"plugin %q already registered as %q\\\", ep.Name, pluginType.Name())\\n548: \\t\\t\\t}\\n549: \\n550: \\t\\t\\t// we only need to update the multipoint set, since we already have the specific extension set from above\\n551: \\t\\t\\tmultiPointEnabled.insert(ep.Name)\\n552: \\t\\t}\\n553: \\n554: \\t\\t// Reorder plugins. Here is the expected order:\\n555: \\t\\t// - part 1: overridePlugins. Their order stay intact as how they're specified in regular extension point.\\n556: \\t\\t// - part 2: multiPointEnabled - i.e., plugin defined in multipoint but not in regular extension point.\\n557: \\t\\t// - part 3: other plugins (excluded by part 1 & 2) in regular extension point.\\n558: \\t\\tnewPlugins := reflect.New(reflect.TypeOf(e.slicePtr).Elem()).Elem()\\n559: \\t\\t// part 1\\n560: reflect.ValueOf(f.pluginsMap[name]))\\n563: \\t\\t\\t\\tenabledSet.delete(name)\\n564: \\t\\t\\t}\\n565: \\t\\t}\\n566: \\t\\t// part 2\\n567: \\t\\tfor _, name := range multiPointEnabled.list \\t\\t\\tnewPlugins = reflect.Append(newPlugins, reflect.ValueOf(f.pluginsMap[name]))\\n573: \\t\\t}\\n574: \\t\\tplugins.Set(newPlugins)\\n575: \\t}\\n576: \\treturn nil\\n577: }\\n578: \\n579: func EnqueueExtensions.\\n582: \\t// See the comment of EnqueueExtensions for more detailed reason here.\\n583: \\tcase framework.PreEnqueuePlugin, framework.PreFilterPlugin, framework.FilterPlugin, framework.ReservePlugin, framework.PermitPlugin:\\n584: \\t\\treturn true\\n585: \\t}\\n586: \\treturn false\\n587: }\\n588: \\n589: func (f *frameworkImpl) fillEnqueueExtensions(p framework.Plugin) Score\n429: // plugin weights there is not an overflow of MaxTotalScore.\n430: func getScoreWeights(f *frameworkImpl, plugins []config.Plugin) error {\n431: \tvar totalPriority int64\n432: \tscorePlugins := reflect.ValueOf(&f.scorePlugins).Elem()\n433: \tpluginType := scorePlugins.Type().Elem()\n434: \tfor _, e := range plugins {\n435: \t\tpg := f.pluginsMap[e.Name]\n436: \t\tif \t\t}\n445: \t\t// a weight of zero is not permitted, plugins can be disabled explicitly\n446: \t\t// when configured.\n447: \t\tf.scorePluginWeight[e.Name] = int(e.Weight)\n448: \t\tif \t\ttotalPriority += int64(f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name]) * framework.MaxNodeScore\n457: \t}\n458: \treturn nil\n459: }\n460: \n461: type orderedSet struct {\n462: \tset         map[string]int\n463: \tlist        []string\n464: \tdeletionCnt int\n465: }\n466: \n467: func newOrderedSet() *orderedSet {\n468: \treturn &orderedSet{set: make(map[string]int)}\n469: }\n470: \n471: func (os *orderedSet) insert(s string) {\n472: \tif os.has(s) {\n473: \t\treturn\n474: \t}\n475: \tos.set[s] = len(os.list)\n476: \tos.list = append(os.list, s)\n477: }\n478: \n479: func (os *orderedSet) has(s string) bool {\n480: \t_, found := os.set[s]\n481: \treturn found\n482: }\n483: \n484: func (os *orderedSet) delete(s string) {\n485: \tif i, found := os.set[s]; found {\n486: \t\tdelete(os.set, s)\n487: \t\tos.list = append(os.list[:i-os.deletionCnt], os.list[i+1-os.deletionCnt:]...)\n488: \t\tos.deletionCnt++\n489: \t}\n490: }\n491: \n492: func (f *frameworkImpl) expandMultiPointPlugins(logger klog.Logger, profile *config.KubeSchedulerProfile) error {\n493: \t// initialize MultiPoint plugins\n494: \tfor _, e := range f.getExtensionPoints(profile.Plugins) {\n495: \t\tplugins := reflect.ValueOf(e.slicePtr).Elem()\n496: \t\tpluginType := plugins.Type().Elem()\n497: \t\t// build enabledSet of plugins already registered via normal extension points\n498: \t\t// to check double registration\n499: \t\tenabledSet := newOrderedSet()\n500: \t\tfor _, plugin := range e.plugins.Enabled {\n501: \t\t\tenabledSet.insert(plugin.Name)\n502: \t\t}\n503: \n504: \t\tdisabledSet := sets.New[string]()\n505: \t\tfor _, disabledPlugin := range e.plugins.Disabled {\n506: \t\t\tdisabledSet.Insert(disabledPlugin.Name)\n507: \t\t}\n508: \t\tif disabledSet.Has(\"*\") {\n509: \t\t\tlogger.V(4).Info(\"Skipped MultiPoint expansion because all plugins are disabled for extension point\", \"extension\", pluginType)\n510: \t\t\tcontinue\n511: \t\t}\n512: \n513: \t\t// track plugins enabled via multipoint separately from those enabled by specific extensions,\n514: \t\t// so that we can distinguish between double-registration and explicit overrides\n515: \t\tmultiPointEnabled := newOrderedSet()\n516: \t\toverridePlugins := newOrderedSet()\n517: \t\tfor _, ep := range profile.Plugins.MultiPoint.Enabled {\n518: \t\t\tpg, ok := f.pluginsMap[ep.Name]\n519: \t\t\tif !ok {\n520: \t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"%s %q does not exist\", pluginType.Name(), ep.Name)\n521: \t\t\t}\n522: \n523: \t\t\t// if this plugin doesn't implement the type for the current extension we're trying to expand, skip\n524: \t\t\tif !reflect.TypeOf(pg).Implements(pluginType) {\n525: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n526: \t\t\t}\n527: \n528: \t\t\t// a plugin that's enabled via MultiPoint can still be disabled for specific extension points\n529: \t\t\tif disabledSet.Has(ep.Name) {\n530: \t\t\t\tlogger.V(4).Info(\"Skipped disabled plugin for extension point\", \"plugin\", ep.Name, \"extension\", pluginType)\n531: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n532: \t\t\t}\n533: \n534: \t\t\t// if this plugin has already been enabled by the specific extension point,\n535: \t\t\t// the user intent is to override the default plugin or make some other explicit setting.\n536: \t\t\t// Either way, discard the MultiPoint value for this plugin.\n537: \t\t\t// This maintains expected behavior for overriding default plugins (see https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/99582)\n538: \t\t\tif enabledSet.has(ep.Name) {\n539: \t\t\t\toverridePlugins.insert(ep.Name)\n540: \t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"MultiPoint plugin is explicitly re-configured; overriding\", \"plugin\", ep.Name)\n541: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n542: \t\t\t}\n543: \n544: \t\t\t// if this plugin is already registered via MultiPoint, then this is\n545: \t\t\t// a double registration and an error in the config.\n546: \t\t\tif multiPointEnabled.has(ep.Name) {\n547: \t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"plugin %q already registered as %q\", ep.Name, pluginType.Name())\n548: \t\t\t}\n549: \n550: \t\t\t// we only need to update the multipoint set, since we already have the specific extension set from above\n551: \t\t\tmultiPointEnabled.insert(ep.Name)\n552: \t\t}\n553: \n554: \t\t// Reorder plugins. Here is the expected order:\n555: \t\t// - part 1: overridePlugins. Their order stay intact as how they're specified in regular extension point.\n556: \t\t// - part 2: multiPointEnabled - i.e., plugin defined in multipoint but not in regular extension point.\n557: \t\t// - part 3: other plugins (excluded by part 1 & 2) in regular extension point.\n558: \t\tnewPlugins := reflect.New(reflect.TypeOf(e.slicePtr).Elem()).Elem()\n559: \t\t// part 1\n560: \t\tfor _, name := range slice.CopyStrings(enabledSet.list) {\n561: \t\t\tif overridePlugins.has(name) {\n562: \t\t\t\tnewPlugins = reflect.Append(newPlugins, reflect.ValueOf(f.pluginsMap[name]))\n563: \t\t\t\tenabledSet.delete(name)\n564: \t\t\t}\n565: \t\t}\n566: \t\t// part 2\n567: \t\tfor _, name := range multiPointEnabled.list {\n568: \t\t\tnewPlugins = reflect.Append(newPlugins, reflect.ValueOf(f.pluginsMap[name]))\n569: \t\t}\n570: \t\t// part 3\n571: \t\tfor _, name := range enabledSet.list {\n572: \t\t\tnewPlugins = reflect.Append(newPlugins, reflect.ValueOf(f.pluginsMap[name]))\n573: \t\t}\n574: \t\tplugins.Set(newPlugins)\n575: \t}\n576: \treturn nil\n577: }\n578: \n579: func shouldHaveEnqueueExtensions(p framework.Plugin) bool {\n580: \tswitch p.(type) {\n581: \t// Only PreEnqueue, PreFilter, Filter, Reserve, and Permit plugins can (should) have EnqueueExtensions.\n582: \t// See the comment of EnqueueExtensions for more detailed reason here.\n583: \tcase framework.PreEnqueuePlugin, framework.PreFilterPlugin, framework.FilterPlugin, framework.ReservePlugin, framework.PermitPlugin:\n584: \t\treturn true\n585: \t}\n586: \treturn false\n587: }\n588: \n589: func (f *frameworkImpl) fillEnqueueExtensions(p framework.Plugin) {\n590: \tif !shouldHaveEnqueueExtensions(p) {\n591: \t\t// Ignore EnqueueExtensions from plugin which isn't PreEnqueue, PreFilter, Filter, Reserve, and Permit.\n592: \t\treturn\n593: \t}\n594: \n595: \text, ok := p.(framework.EnqueueExtensions)\n596: \tif !ok {\n597: \t\t// If interface {\\n680: \\tstartTime := time.Now()\\n681: \\tskipPlugins := sets.New[string]()\\n682: \\tdefer func() {\\n683: \\t\\tstate.SkipFilterPlugins = skipPlugins\\n684: // RunPostFilterPlugins runs the set of configured PostFilter plugins until the first\\n888: // Success, Error or UnschedulableAndUnresolvable is met; otherwise continues to execute all plugins.\\n889: func (f *frameworkImpl) RunPostFilterPlugins(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, filteredNodeStatusMap framework.NodeToStatusMap) (_ *framework.PostFilterResult, status *framework.Status) {\\n890: \\tstartTime := time.Now()\\n891: \\tdefer func() {\\n892: \\t\\tmetrics.FrameworkExtensionPointDuration.WithLabelValues(metrics.PostFilter, status.Code().String(), f.profileName).Observe(metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\\n893: \\t}()\\n894: \\n895: \\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\\n896: \\tverboseLogs := logger.V(4).Enabled()\\n897: \\tif verboseLogs {\\n898: \\t\\tlogger = klog.LoggerWithName(logger, \\\"PostFilter\\\")\\n899: \\t}\\n900: \\n901: \\t// `result` records the last meaningful(non-noop) PostFilterResult.\\n902: \\tvar result *framework.PostFilterResult\\n903: \\tvar reasons []string\\n904: \\tvar rejectorPlugin string\\n905: \\tfor _, pl := range f.postFilterPlugins {\\n906: \\t\\tctx := ctx\\n907: \\t\\tif verboseLogs {\\n908: \\t\\t\\tlogger := klog.LoggerWithName(logger, pl.Name())\\n909: \\t\\t\\tctx =
