framework.PostFilterPlugin, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, filteredNodeStatusMap framework.NodeToStatusMap) (*framework.PostFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\\n935: \\tif !state.ShouldRecordPluginMetrics() {\\n936: \\t\\treturn pl.PostFilter(ctx, state, pod, filteredNodeStatusMap)\\n937: \\t}\\n938: \\tstartTime := time.Now()\\n939: \\tr, s := pl.PostFilter(ctx, state, pod, filteredNodeStatusMap)\\n940: \\tf.metricsRecorder.ObservePluginDurationAsync(metrics.PostFilter, pl.Name(), s.Code().String(), metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\\n941: \\treturn r, s\\n942: }\\n943: \\n944: // RunFilterPluginsWithNominatedPods runs the set of configured filter plugins\\n945: // for nominated pod on the given node.\\n946: // This function is called from two different places: Schedule and Preempt.\\n947: // When it is called from Schedule, we want to test whether the pod is\\n948: // schedulable on the node with all the existing pods on the node plus higher\\n949: // and equal priority pods nominated to run on the node.\\n950: // When it is called from Preempt, we should remove the victims of preemption\\n951: // and add the nominated pods. Removal of the victims is done by\\n952: // SelectVictimsOnNode(). Preempt removes victims from PreFilter state and\\n953: // NodeInfo before calling this function.\\n954: func (f *frameworkImpl) RunFilterPluginsWithNominatedPods(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, info *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\\n955: \\tvar status *framework.Status\\n956: \\n957: \\tpodsAdded := false\\n958: \\t// We run filters twice in some cases. If the node has greater or equal priority\\n959: \\t// nominated pods, we run them when those pods are added to PreFilter state and nodeInfo.\\n960: \\t// If all filters succeed in this pass, we run them again when these\\n961: \\t// nominated pods are not added. This second pass is necessary because some\\n962: \\t// filters such as inter-pod affinity may not pass without the nominated pods.\\n963: \\t// If there are no nominated pods for the node or if the first run of the\\n964: \\t// filters fail, we don't run the second pass.\\n965: \\t// We consider only equal or higher priority pods in the first pass, because\\n966: \\t// those are the current \\\"pod\\\" must yield to them and not take a space opened\\n967: \\t// for running them. It is ok if the current \\\"pod\\\" take resources freed for\\n968: \\t// lower priority pods.\\n969: \\t// Requiring that the new pod is schedulable in both circumstances ensures that\\n970: \\t// we are making a conservative decision: filters like resources and inter-pod\\n971: \\t// anti-affinity are more likely to fail when the nominated pods are treated\\n972: \\t// as running, while filters like pod affinity are more likely to fail when\\n973: \\t// the nominated pods are treated as not running. We can't just assume the\\n974: \\t// nominated pods are running because they are not running right now and in fact,\\n975: \\t// they may end up getting scheduled to a different node.\\n976: \\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\\n977: \\tlogger = klog.LoggerWithName(logger, \\\"FilterWithNominatedPods\\\")\\n978: \\tctx = klog.NewContext(ctx, logger)\\n979: \\tfor i := 0; i < 2; i++ {\\n980: \\t\\tstateToUse := state\\n981: \\t\\tnodeInfoToUse := info\\n982: \\t\\tif i == 0 {\\n983: \\t\\t\\tvar err error\\n984: \\t\\t\\tpodsAdded, stateToUse, nodeInfoToUse, err = addNominatedPods(ctx, f, pod, state, info)\\n985: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n986: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\\n987: \\t\\t\\t}\\n988: \\t\\t} else if !podsAdded || !status.IsSuccess() {\\n989: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n990: \\t\\t}\\n991: \\n992: \\t\\tstatus = f.RunFilterPlugins(ctx, stateToUse, pod, nodeInfoToUse)\\n993: \\t\\tif !status.IsSuccess() && !status.IsRejected() {\\n994: \\t\\t\\treturn status\\n995: \\t\\t}\\n996: \\t}\\n997: \\n998: \\treturn status\\n999: }\\n1000: \\n1001: // addNominatedPods adds pods with equal or greater priority which are nominated\\n1002: // to run on the node. It returns 1) whether any pod was added, 2) augmented cycleState,\\n1003: // 3) augmented nodeInfo.\\n1004: func addNominatedPods(ctx context.Context, fh framework.Handle, pod *v1.Pod, state *framework.CycleState, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) (bool, *framework.CycleState, *framework.NodeInfo, error) {\\n1005: \\tif fh == nil {\\n1006: \\t\\t// This may happen only in tests.\\n1007: \\t\\treturn false, state, nodeInfo, nil\\n1008: \\t}\\n1009: \\tnominatedPodInfos := fh.NominatedPodsForNode(nodeInfo.Node().Name)\\n1010: \\tif len(nominatedPodInfos) == 0 {\\n1011: \\t\\treturn false, state, nodeInfo, nil\\n1012: \\t}\\n1013: \\tnodeInfoOut := nodeInfo.Snapshot()\\n1014: \\tstateOut := state.Clone()\\n1015: \\tpodsAdded := false\\n1016: \\tfor _, pi := range nominatedPodInfos {\\n1017: \\t\\tif corev1.PodPriority(pi.Pod) >= corev1.PodPriority(pod) && pi.Pod.UID != pod.UID {\\n1018: \\t\\t\\tnodeInfoOut.AddPodInfo(pi)\\n1019: \\t\\t\\tstatus := fh.RunPreFilterExtensionAddPod(ctx, stateOut, pod, pi, nodeInfoOut)\\n1020: \\t\\t\\tif !status.IsSuccess() {\\n1021: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn false, state, nodeInfo, status.AsError()\\n1022: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1023: \\t\\t\\tpodsAdded = true\\n1024: \\t\\t}\\n1025: \\t}\\n1026: \\treturn podsAdded, stateOut, nodeInfoOut, nil\\n1027: }\\n1028: \\n1029: // RunPreScorePlugins runs the set of configured pre-score plugins. If any\\n1030: // of these plugins returns any status other than Success/Skip, the given pod is rejected.\\n1031: // When it returns Skip status, other fields in status are just ignored,\\n1032: // and coupled Score plugin will be skipped in this scheduling cycle.\\n1033: func (f *frameworkImpl) RunPreScorePlugins(\\n1034: \\tctx context.Context,\\n1035: \\tstate *framework.CycleState,\\n1036: \\tpod *v1.Pod,\\n1037: \\tnodes []*framework.NodeInfo,\\n1038: ) (status *framework.Status) {\\n1039: \\tstartTime := time.Now()\\n1040: \\tskipPlugins := sets.New[string]()\\n1041: \\tdefer func() {\\n1042: \\t\\tstate.SkipScorePlugins = skipPlugins\\n1043: \\t\\tmetrics.FrameworkExtensionPointDuration.WithLabelValues(metrics.PreScore, status.Code().String(), f.profileName).Observe(metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\\n1044: \\t}()\\n1045: \\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\\n1046: \\tverboseLogs := logger.V(4).Enabled()\\n1047: \\tif verboseLogs {\\n1048: plugins.\\n889: func (f *frameworkImpl) RunPostFilterPlugins(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, filteredNodeStatusMap framework.NodeToStatusMap) (_ *framework.PostFilterResult, status *framework.Status) {\\n890: \\tstartTime := time.Now()\\n891: \\tdefer func() {\\n892: \\t\\tmetrics.FrameworkExtensionPointDuration.WithLabelValues(metrics.PostFilter, status.Code().String(), f.profileName).Observe(metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\\n893: \\t}()\\n894: \\n895: \\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\\n896: \\tverboseLogs := logger.V(4).Enabled()\\n897: \\tif verboseLogs {\\n898: \\t\\tlogger = klog.LoggerWithName(logger, \\\"PostFilter\\\")\\n899: \\t}\\n900: \\n901: \\t// `result` records the last meaningful(non-noop) PostFilterResult.\\n902: \\tvar result *framework.PostFilterResult\\n903: \\tvar reasons []string\\n904: \\tvar rejectorPlugin string\\n905: \\tfor _, pl := range f.postFilterPlugins {\\n906: \\t\\tctx := ctx\\n907: \\t\\tif verboseLogs {\\n908: \\t\\t\\tlogger := klog.LoggerWithName(logger, pl.Name())\\n909: \\t\\t\\tctx = klog.NewContext(ctx, logger)\\n910: \\t\\t}\\n911: \\t\\tr, s := f.runPostFilterPlugin(ctx, pl, state, pod, filteredNodeStatusMap)\\n912: \\t\\tif s.IsSuccess() {\\n913: \\t\\t\\treturn r, s\\n914: \\t\\t} else if s.Code() == framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable {\\n915: \\t\\t\\treturn r, s.WithPlugin(pl.Name())\\n916: \\t\\t} else if !s.IsRejected() {\\n917: \\t\\t\\t// Any status other than Success, Unschedulable or UnschedulableAndUnresolvable is Error.\\n918: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, framework.AsStatus(s.AsError()).WithPlugin(pl.Name())\\n919: \\t\\t} else if r != nil && r.Mode() != that\\n925: \\t\\t// the latter is more relevant.\\n926: \\t\\tif len(rejectorPlugin) == 0 {\\n927: \\t\\t\\trejectorPlugin = pl.Name()\\n928: \\t\\t}\\n929: \\t}\\n930: \\n931: \\treturn result, framework.NewStatus(framework.Unschedulable, reasons...).WithPlugin(rejectorPlugin)\\n932: }\\n933: \\n934: func (f *frameworkImpl) runPostFilterPlugin(ctx context.Context, pl framework.PostFilterPlugin, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, filteredNodeStatusMap framework.NodeToStatusMap) (*framework.PostFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\\n935: \\tif !state.ShouldRecordPluginMetrics() {\\n936: \\t\\treturn pl.PostFilter(ctx, state, pod, filteredNodeStatusMap)\\n937: \\t}\\n938: \\tstartTime := time.Now()\\n939: \\tr, s := pl.PostFilter(ctx, state, }\\n943: \\n944: // RunFilterPluginsWithNominatedPods runs the set of configured filter plugins\\n945: // for nominated pod on the given node.\\n946: // This function is called from two different places: Schedule and Preempt.\\n947: // When it is called from Schedule, we want to test whether the pod is\\n948: // schedulable on the node with all the existing pods on the node plus higher\\n949: // and equal priority pods nominated to run on the node.\\n950: // When it is called from Preempt, we should remove the victims of preemption\\n951: // and add the nominated pods. Removal of the victims is done by\\n952: // SelectVictimsOnNode(). Preempt removes victims from PreFilter state and\\n953: // NodeInfo before calling this function.\\n954: func (f *frameworkImpl) RunFilterPluginsWithNominatedPods(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, info *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\\n955: \\tvar status *framework.Status\\n956: \\n957: \\tpodsAdded := false\\n958: \\t// We run filters twice in some cases. If the node has greater or equal priority\\n959: \\t// nominated pods, we run them when those pods are added to PreFilter state and nodeInfo.\\n960: \\t// If all filters succeed in this pass, we run them again when these\\n961: \\t// nominated pods are not added. This second pass is necessary because some\\n962: \\t// filters such as inter-pod affinity may not pass without the nominated pods.\\n963: \\t// If there are no nominated pods for the node or if the first run of the\\n964: \\t// filters fail, we don't run the second pass.\\n965: \\t// We consider only equal or higher priority pods in the first pass, because\\n966: \\t// those are the current \\\"pod\\\" must yield to them and not take a space opened\\n967: \\t// for running them. It is ok if the current \\\"pod\\\" take resources freed for\\n968: \\t// lower priority pods.\\n969: \\t// Requiring that the new pod is schedulable in both circumstances ensures that\\n970: \\t// we are making a conservative decision: filters like resources and inter-pod\\n971: \\t// anti-affinity are more likely to fail when the nominated pods are treated\\n972: \\t// as running, while filters like pod affinity are more likely to fail when\\n973: \\t// the nominated pods are treated as not running. We can't just assume the\\n974: \\t// nominated pods are running because they are not running right now and in fact,\\n975: \\t// they may end up getting scheduled to a different node.\\n976: \\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\\n977: \\tlogger = klog.LoggerWithName(logger, \\\"FilterWithNominatedPods\\\")\\n978: \\tctx = klog.NewContext(ctx, logger)\\n979: \\tfor i := 0; i < 2; i++ {\\n980: \\t\\tstateToUse := state\\n981: \\t\\tnodeInfoToUse := info\\n982: \\t\\tif i == 0 {\\n983: \\t\\t\\tvar err error\\n984: \\t\\t\\tpodsAdded, stateToUse, nodeInfoToUse, err = addNominatedPods(ctx, f, pod, state, info)\\n985: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n986: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\\n987: \\t\\t\\t}\\n988: \\t\\t} else if !podsAdded || !status.IsSuccess() {\\n989: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n990: \\t\\t}\\n991: \\n992: \\t\\tstatus = f.RunFilterPlugins(ctx, stateToUse, pod, nodeInfoToUse)\\n993: \\t\\tif !status.IsSuccess() && !status.IsRejected() {\\n994: \\t\\t\\treturn status\\n995: \\t\\t}\\n996: \\t}\\n997: \\n998: \\treturn status\\n999: }\\n1000: \\n1001: // addNominatedPods adds pods with equal or greater priority which are nominated\\n1002: // to run on the node. It returns 1) whether any pod was added, 2) augmented cycleState,\\n1003: // 3) augmented nodeInfo.\\n1004: func addNominatedPods(ctx context.Context, fh framework.Handle, pod *v1.Pod, state *framework.CycleState, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) (bool, *framework.CycleState, *framework.NodeInfo, error) {\\n1005: \\tif fh == nil {\\n1006: \\t\\t// This may happen only in tests.\\n1007: \\t\\treturn false, state, nodeInfo, nil\\n1008: \\t}\\n1009: \\tnominatedPodInfos := fh.NominatedPodsForNode(nodeInfo.Node().Name)\\n1010: \\tif len(nominatedPodInfos) == 0 {\\n1011: \\t\\treturn false, state, nodeInfo, nil\\n1012: \\t}\\n1013: \\tnodeInfoOut := nodeInfo.Snapshot()\\n1014: \\tstateOut := state.Clone()\\n1015: \\tpodsAdded := false\\n1016: \\tfor _, pi := range nominatedPodInfos {\\n1017: \\t\\tif corev1.PodPriority(pi.Pod) >= corev1.PodPriority(pod) && pi.Pod.UID != pod.UID {\\n1018: \\t\\t\\tnodeInfoOut.AddPodInfo(pi)\\n1019: \\t\\t\\tstatus := fh.RunPreFilterExtensionAddPod(ctx, stateOut, pod, pi, nodeInfoOut)\\n1020: \\t\\t\\tif !status.IsSuccess() {\\n1021: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn false, state, nodeInfo, status.AsError()\\n1022: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1023: \\t\\t\\tpodsAdded = true\\n1024: \\t\\t}\\n1025: \\t}\\n1026: \\treturn podsAdded, stateOut, nodeInfoOut, nil\\n1027: }\\n1028: \\n1029: // RunPreScorePlugins runs the set of configured pre-score plugins. and coupled Score plugin will be skipped in this scheduling cycle.\\n1033: func (f *frameworkImpl) RunPreScorePlugins(\\n1034: \\tctx context.Context,\\n1035: \\tstate *framework.CycleState,\\n1036: satisfy plugin(s) %v simultaneously\", pluginsWithNodes)\n727: \t\t\tif len(pluginsWithNodes) == 1 {\n728: \t\t\t\tmsg = fmt.Sprintf(\"node(s) didn't satisfy plugin %v\", pluginsWithNodes[0])\n729: \t\t\t}\n730: \n731: \t\t\t// When PreFilterResult filters out Nodes, the framework considers Nodes that are filtered out as getting \"UnschedulableAndUnresolvable\".\n732: \t\t\treturn result, framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, msg)\n733: \t\t}\n734: \t}\n735: \treturn result, returnStatus\n736: }\n737: \n738: func (f *frameworkImpl) runPreFilterPlugin(ctx status\n885: }\n886: \n887: // RunPostFilterPlugins runs the set of configured PostFilter plugins until the first\n888: // Success, Error or UnschedulableAndUnresolvable is met; otherwise continues to execute all plugins.\n889: func (f *frameworkImpl) RunPostFilterPlugins(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, filteredNodeStatusMap framework.NodeToStatusMap) (_ *framework.PostFilterResult, status *framework.Status) {\n890: \tstartTime := time.Now()\n891: \tdefer func() {\n892: \t\tmetrics.FrameworkExtensionPointDuration.WithLabelValues(metrics.PostFilter, status.Code().String(), f.profileName).Observe(metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\n893: \t}()\n894: \n895: \tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n896: \tverboseLogs := logger.V(4).Enabled()\n897: \tif verboseLogs {\n898: \t\tlogger = klog.LoggerWithName(logger, \"PostFilter\")\n899: \t}\n900: \n901: \t// `result` records the last meaningful(non-noop) PostFilterResult.\n902: \tvar result *framework.PostFilterResult\n903: \tvar reasons []string\n904: \tvar rejectorPlugin string\n905: \tfor _, pl := range f.postFilterPlugins {\n906: \t\tctx := ctx\n907: \t\tif verboseLogs {\n908: filteredNodeStatusMap)\n912: \t\tif s.IsSuccess() {\n913: \t\t\treturn r, s\n914: \t\t} else if s.Code() == framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable {\n915: \t\t\treturn r, s.WithPlugin(pl.Name())\n916: \t\t} else if !s.IsRejected() {\n917: \t\t\t// Any status other than Success, Unschedulable or UnschedulableAndUnresolvable is Error.\n918: \t\t\treturn nil, framework.AsStatus(s.AsError()).WithPlugin(pl.Name())\n919: \t\t} else if r != nil && r.Mode() != framework.ModeNoop {\n920: \t\t\tresult = r\n921: \t\t}\n922: \n923: \t\treasons = append(reasons, s.Reasons()...)\n924: \t\t// Record the first failed plugin unless we proved that\n925: \t\t// the latter is more relevant.\n926: \t\tif len(rejectorPlugin) == 0 {\n927: \t\t\trejectorPlugin = pl.Name()\n928: \t\t}\n929: \t}\n930: \n931: \treturn result, framework.NewStatus(framework.Unschedulable, reasons...).WithPlugin(rejectorPlugin)\n932: }\n933: \n934: func (f *frameworkImpl) runPostFilterPlugin(ctx context.Context, pl framework.PostFilterPlugin, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, filteredNodeStatusMap framework.NodeToStatusMap) (*framework.PostFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\n935: \tif !state.ShouldRecordPluginMetrics() {\n936: \t\treturn pl.PostFilter(ctx, state, pod, filteredNodeStatusMap)\n937: \t}\n938: \tstartTime := time.Now()\n939: \tr, s := pl.PostFilter(ctx, state, pod, filteredNodeStatusMap)\n940: \tf.metricsRecorder.ObservePluginDurationAsync(metrics.PostFilter, pl.Name(), s.Code().String(), metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\n941: \treturn r, s\n942: }\n943: \n944: // RunFilterPluginsWithNominatedPods runs the set of configured filter plugins\n945: // for nominated pod on the given node.\n946: // This function is called from two different places: Schedule and Preempt.\n947: // When it is called from Schedule, we want to test whether the pod is\n948: // schedulable on the node with all the existing pods on the node plus higher\n949: // and equal priority pods nominated to run on the node.\n950: // When it is called from Preempt, we should remove the victims of preemption\n951: // and add the nominated pods. Removal of the victims is done by\n952: // SelectVictimsOnNode(). Preempt removes victims from PreFilter state and\n953: // NodeInfo before calling this function.\n954: func (f *frameworkImpl) RunFilterPluginsWithNominatedPods(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, info *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n955: \tvar status *framework.Status\n956: \n957: \tpodsAdded := false\n958: \t// We run filters twice in some cases. If the node has greater or equal priority\n959: \t// nominated pods, we run them when those pods are added to PreFilter state and nodeInfo.\n960: \t// If all filters succeed in this pass, we run them again when these\n961: \t// nominated pods are not added. This second pass is necessary because some\n962: \t// filters such as inter-pod affinity may not pass without the nominated pods.\n963: \t// If there are no nominated pods for the node or if the first run of the\n964: \t// filters fail, we don't run the second pass.\n965: \t// We consider only equal or higher priority pods in the first pass, because\n966: \t// those are the current \"pod\" must yield to them and not take a space opened\n967: \t// for running them. It is ok if the current \"pod\" take resources freed for\n968: \t// lower priority pods.\n969: \t// Requiring that the new pod is schedulable in both circumstances ensures that\n970: \t// we are making a conservative decision: filters like resources and inter-pod\n971: \t// anti-affinity are more likely to fail when the nominated pods are treated\n972: \t// as running, while filters like pod affinity are more likely to fail when\n973: \t// the nominated pods are treated as not running. We can't just assume the\n974: \t// nominated pods are running because they are not running right now and in fact,\n975: \t// they may end up getting scheduled to a different node.\n976: \tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n977: \tlogger = klog.LoggerWithName(logger, \"FilterWithNominatedPods\")\n978: \tctx = klog.NewContext(ctx, logger)\n979: \tfor i := 0; i < 2; i++ {\n980: \t\tstateToUse := state\n981: \t\tnodeInfoToUse := info\n982: \t\tif i == 0 {\n983: \t\t\tvar err error\n984: \t\t\tpodsAdded, stateToUse, nodeInfoToUse, err = addNominatedPods(ctx, f, pod, state, info)\n985: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n986: \t\t\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n987: \t\t\t}\n988: \t\t} else if !podsAdded || !status.IsSuccess() {\n989: \t\t\tbreak\n990: \t\t}\n991: \n992: \t\tstatus = f.RunFilterPlugins(ctx, stateToUse, pod, nodeInfoToUse)\n993: \t\tif !status.IsSuccess() && !status.IsRejected() {\n994: \t\t\treturn status\n995: \t\t}\n996: \t}\n997: \n998: \treturn status\n999: }\n1000: \n1001: // addNominatedPods adds pods with equal or greater priority which are nominated\n1002: // to run on the node. It returns 1) whether any pod was added, 2) augmented cycleState,\n1003: // 3) augmented nodeInfo.\n1004: func addNominatedPods(ctx context.Context, fh framework.Handle, pod *v1.Pod, state *framework.CycleState, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) (bool, *framework.CycleState, *framework.NodeInfo, error) {\n1005: \tif fh == nil {\n1006: \t\t// This may happen only in tests.\n1007: \t\treturn false, state, nodeInfo, nil\n1008: \t}\n1009: \tnominatedPodInfos := fh.NominatedPodsForNode(nodeInfo.Node().Name)\n1010: \tif len(nominatedPodInfos) == 0 {\n1011: \t\treturn false, state, nodeInfo, nil\n1012: \t}\n1013: \tnodeInfoOut := nodeInfo.Snapshot()\n1014: \tstateOut := state.Clone()\n1015: \tpodsAdded := false\n1016: \tfor _, pi := range nominatedPodInfos {\n1017: \t\tif corev1.PodPriority(pi.Pod) >= corev1.PodPriority(pod) && pi.Pod.UID != pod.UID {\n1018: \t\t\tnodeInfoOut.AddPodInfo(pi)\n1019: \t\t\tstatus := fh.RunPreFilterExtensionAddPod(ctx, stateOut, pod, pi, nodeInfoOut)\n1020: \t\t\tif !status.IsSuccess() {\n1021: \t\t\t\treturn false, state, nodeInfo, status.AsError()\n1022: \t\t\t}\n1023: \t\t\tpodsAdded = true\n1024: \t\t}\n1025: \t}\n1026: \treturn podsAdded, stateOut, nodeInfoOut, nil\n1027: }\n1028: \n1029: // RunPreScorePlugins runs the set of configured pre-score plugins. If any\n1030: // of these plugins returns any status other than Success/Skip, the given pod is rejected.\n1031: // When it returns Skip status, other fields in status are just ignored,\n1032: // and coupled Score plugin will be skipped in this scheduling cycle.\n1033: func (f *frameworkImpl) RunPreScorePlugins(\n1034: \tctx context.Context,\n1035: \tstate *framework.CycleState,\n1036: \tpod *v1.Pod,\n1037: \tnodes []*framework.NodeInfo,\n1038: ) (status *framework.Status) {\n1039: \tstartTime := time.Now()\n1040: \tskipPlugins := sets.New[string]()\n1041: \tdefer func() {\n1042: f.profileName).Observe(metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\n1044: \t}()\n1045: \tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n1046: \tverboseLogs := logger.V(4).Enabled()\n1047: \tif verboseLogs {\n1048: {\n1058: \t\t\tskipPlugins.Insert(pl.Name())\n1059: \t\t\tcontinue\n1060: \t\t}\n1061: \t\tif !status.IsSuccess() {\n1062: \t\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"running PreScore plugin %q: %w\", pl.Name(), status.AsError()))\n1063: \t\t}\n1064: \t}\n1065: \treturn nil\n1066: }\n1067: \n1068: func (f *frameworkImpl) runPreScorePlugin(ctx context.Context, pl framework.PreScorePlugin, \t}\n1072: \tstartTime := time.Now()\n1073: \tstatus := pl.PreScore(ctx, state, pod, nodes)\n1074: \tf.metricsRecorder.ObservePluginDurationAsync(metrics.PreScore, pl.Name(), status.Code().String(), RunScorePlugins(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodes []*framework.NodeInfo) (ns []framework.NodePluginScores, status *framework.Status) {\n1083: \tstartTime := {\n1153: \t\treturn nil, framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"running Normalize on Score plugins: %w\", err))\n1154: \t}\n1155: \n1156: \t// Apply score weight for each ScorePlugin in parallel,\n1157: \t// and then, build allNodePluginScores.\n1158: \tf.Parallelizer().Until(ctx, len(nodes), func(index int) {\n1159: \t\tnodePluginScores := framework.NodePluginScores{\n1160: \t\t\tName: There is no guarantee that the information\n1513: // remains unchanged after \"Reserve\".\n1514: func (f *frameworkImpl) SnapshotSharedLister() framework.SharedLister {\n1515: \treturn f.snapshotSharedLister\n1516: }\n1517: \n1518: // IterateOverWaitingPods acquires a read lock and iterates over the WaitingPods map.\n1519: func (f *frameworkImpl) IterateOverWaitingPods(callback func(framework.WaitingPod)) {\n1520: \tf.waitingPods.iterate(callback)\n1521: }\n1522: \n1523: // GetWaitingPod returns a reference to a WaitingPod given its UID.\n1524: func (f *frameworkImpl) GetWaitingPod(uid types.UID) framework.WaitingPod {\n1525: \tif wp := f.waitingPods.get(uid); wp != nil {\n1526: \t\treturn wp\n1527: \t}\n1528: \treturn nil // Returning nil instead of *waitingPod(nil).\n1529: }\n1530: \n1531: // RejectWaitingPod rejects a WaitingPod given its UID.\n1532: // The returned value indicates if the given pod is waiting or not.\n1533: func (f *frameworkImpl) RejectWaitingPod(uid types.UID) bool {\n1534: \tif waitingPod := f.waitingPods.get(uid); waitingPod != nil {\n1535: \t\twaitingPod.Reject(\"\", \"removed\")\n1536: \t\treturn true\n1537: \t}\n1538: \treturn false\n1539: }\n1540: \n1541: // HasFilterPlugins returns true if at least one filter plugin is defined.\n1542: func (f *frameworkImpl) HasFilterPlugins() bool {\n1543: \treturn len(f.filterPlugins) > 0\n1544: }\n1545: \n1546: // HasPostFilterPlugins returns true if at least one postFilter plugin is defined.\n1547: func (f *frameworkImpl) HasPostFilterPlugins() bool {\n1548: \treturn len(f.postFilterPlugins) > 0\n1549: }\n1550: \n1551: // HasScorePlugins returns true if at least one score plugin is defined.\n1552: func (f *frameworkImpl) HasScorePlugins() bool {\n1553: \treturn len(f.scorePlugins) > 0\n1554: }\n1555: \n1556: // ListPlugins returns a map of extension point name to plugin names configured at each extension\n1557: // point. The interface has only one method (`NormalizeScore`), making the current generic name misleading.\n\n### Scope\n- Rename interface: `ScoreExtensions` → `ScoreNormalizer`\n- Rename accessor method: `ScoreExtensions()` → `ScoreNormalizer()` on the `ScorePlugin` interface\n- Rename metrics constant: `ScoreExtensionNormalize` → `ScoreNormalize`\n\n## Files Examined\n\n### Core Definition\n- **pkg/scheduler/framework/interface.go** — Contains the primary definition of `ScoreExtensions` interface and the `ScoreExtensions()` accessor method on `ScorePlugin` interface. Uses `metrics.ScoreExtensionNormalize` constant for metrics recording. A successful run of NormalizeScore will update the scores list and return\n \t// a success status.\n \tNormalizeScore(ctx context.Context, state *CycleState, p *v1.Pod, scores NodeScoreList) *Status\n }\n\n // ScorePlugin is an interface that must be implemented by \"Score\" plugins to rank\n // nodes that passed the filtering phase.\n type ScorePlugin interface {\n \tPlugin\n \t// Score is called on each filtered node. It must return success and an integer\n \t// indicating the rank of the node. All scoring plugins must return success or\n \t// the pod will be rejected.\n \tScore(ctx context.Context, state *CycleState, p *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *Status)\n\n-\t// ScoreExtensions returns a ScoreExtensions interface if it implements one, or nil if does not.\n-\tScoreExtensions() ScoreExtensions\n+\t// ScoreNormalizer returns a ScoreNormalizer interface if it implements one, or nil if does not.\n+\tScoreNormalizer() ScoreNormalizer\n }\n```\n\n### 2. pkg/scheduler/metrics/metrics.go\n\n**Changes:**\n- Rename metric constant from `ScoreExtensionNormalize` to `ScoreNormalize`\n\n```diff\n \tPreFilterExtensionAddPod    = \"PreFilterExtensionAddPod\"\n \tPreFilterExtensionRemovePod = \"PreFilterExtensionRemovePod\"\n \tPostFilter                  = nodeScoreList)\n-\tf.metricsRecorder.ObservePluginDurationAsync(metrics.ScoreExtensionNormalize, pl.Name(), status.Code().String(), metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\n+\tstatus := metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\n \treturn status\n }\n```\n\n### 4-13. }\\n943: \\n944: // RunFilterPluginsWithNominatedPods runs the set of configured filter plugins\\n945: // for nominated pod on the given node.\\n946: // This function is called from two different places: Schedule and Preempt.\\n947: // When it is called from Schedule, we want to test whether the pod is\\n948: // schedulable on the node with all the existing pods on the node plus higher\\n949: // and equal priority pods nominated to run on the node.\\n950: // When it is called from Preempt, we should remove the victims of preemption\\n951: // and add the nominated pods. Removal of the victims is done by\\n952: // SelectVictimsOnNode(). Preempt removes victims from PreFilter state and\\n953: // NodeInfo before calling this function.\\n954: func (f *frameworkImpl) RunFilterPluginsWithNominatedPods(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, info *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\\n955: \\tvar status *framework.Status\\n956: \\n957: \\tpodsAdded := false\\n958: \\t// We run filters twice in some cases. If the node has greater or equal priority\\n959: \\t// nominated pods, we run them when those pods are added to PreFilter state and nodeInfo.\\n960: \\t// If all filters succeed in this pass, we run them again when these\\n961: \\t// nominated pods are not added. This second pass is necessary because some\\n962: \\t// filters such as inter-pod affinity may not pass without the nominated pods.\\n963: \\t// If there are no nominated pods for the node or if the first run of the\\n964: \\t// filters fail, we don't run the second pass.\\n965: \\t// We consider only equal or higher priority pods in the first pass, because\\n966: \\t// those are the current \\\"pod\\\" must yield to them and not take a space opened\\n967: \\t// for running them. It is ok if the current \\\"pod\\\" take resources freed for\\n968: \\t// lower priority pods.\\n969: \\t// Requiring that the new pod is schedulable in both circumstances ensures that\\n970: \\t// we are making a conservative decision: filters like resources and inter-pod\\n971: \\t// anti-affinity are more likely to fail when the nominated pods are treated\\n972: \\t// as running, while filters like pod affinity are more likely to fail when\\n973: \\t// the nominated pods are treated as not running. The interface has only one method (`NormalizeScore`), making the current generic name misleading.\n\n### Scope\n- Rename interface: `ScoreExtensions` → `ScoreNormalizer`\n- Rename accessor method: `ScoreExtensions()` → `ScoreNormalizer()` on the `ScorePlugin` interface\n- Rename metrics constant: `ScoreExtensionNormalize` → `ScoreNormalize`\n\n## Files Examined\n\n### Core Definition\n- **pkg/scheduler/framework/interface.go** — Contains the primary definition of `ScoreExtensions` interface and the `ScoreExtensions()` accessor method on `ScorePlugin` interface. Uses `metrics.ScoreExtensionNormalize` constant for metrics recording. Contains the `runScoreExtension()` method that invokes `pl.ScoreExtensions().NormalizeScore()`.\n\n### Plugin Implementations (12 files)\nPlugins that implement `ScoreExtensions` interface must be A successful run of NormalizeScore will update the scores list and return\n \t// a success status.\n \tNormalizeScore(ctx context.Context, state *CycleState, p *v1.Pod, scores NodeScoreList) *Status\n }\n\n // ScorePlugin is an interface that must be implemented by \"Score\" plugins to rank\n // nodes that passed the filtering phase.\n type ScorePlugin interface {\n \tPlugin\n \t// Score is called on each filtered node. It must return success and an integer\n \t// indicating the rank of the node. All scoring plugins must return success or\n \t// the pod will be rejected.\n \tScore(ctx context.Context, state *CycleState, p *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *Status)\n\n-\t// ScoreExtensions returns a ScoreExtensions interface if it implements one, or nil if does not.\n-\tScoreExtensions() ScoreExtensions\n+\t// ScoreNormalizer returns a ScoreNormalizer interface if it implements one, or nil if does not.\n+\tScoreNormalizer() ScoreNormalizer\n }\n```\n\n### 2. pkg/scheduler/metrics/metrics.go\n\n**Changes:**\n- Rename metric constant from `ScoreExtensionNormalize` to `ScoreNormalize`\n\n```diff\n \tPreFilterExtensionAddPod    = \"PreFilterExtensionAddPod\"\n \tPreFilterExtensionRemovePod = \"PreFilterExtensionRemovePod\"\n \tPostFilter                  = \"PostFilter\"\n \tPreScore                    = \"PreScore\"\n \tScore                       = \"Score\"\n-\tScoreExtensionNormalize     = \"ScoreExtensionNormalize\"\n+\tScoreNormalize              = \"ScoreNormalize\"\n \tPreBind                     = \"PreBind\"\n \tBind                        = \"Bind\"\n \tPostBind                    = \"PostBind\"\n```\n\n### 3. Any external plugins that implement the `ScorePlugin` interface must be updated.\n\n**Scope**: The change is well-contained within the scheduler framework package and its plugins. The interface has only one method (`NormalizeScore`), making the current generic name misleading.\n\n### Scope\n- Rename interface: `ScoreExtensions` → `ScoreNormalizer`\n- Rename accessor method: `ScoreExtensions()` → `ScoreNormalizer()` on the `ScorePlugin` interface\n- Rename metrics constant: `ScoreExtensionNormalize` → `ScoreNormalize`\n\n## Files Examined\n\n### Core Definition\n- **pkg/scheduler/framework/interface.go** — Contains the primary definition of `ScoreExtensions` interface and the `ScoreExtensions()` accessor method on `ScorePlugin` interface. A successful run of NormalizeScore will update the scores list and return\n \t// a success status.\n \tNormalizeScore(ctx context.Context, state *CycleState, p *v1.Pod, scores NodeScoreList) *Status\n }\n\n // ScorePlugin is an interface that must be implemented by \"Score\" plugins to rank\n // nodes that passed the filtering phase.\n type ScorePlugin interface {\n \tPlugin\n \t// Score is called on each filtered node. It must return success and an integer\n \t// indicating the rank of the node. All scoring plugins must return success or\n \t// the pod will be rejected.\n \tScore(ctx context.Context, state *CycleState, p *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *Status)\n\n-\t// ScoreExtensions returns a ScoreExtensions interface if it implements one, or nil if does not.\n-\tScoreExtensions() ScoreExtensions\n+\t// ScoreNormalizer returns a ScoreNormalizer interface if it implements one, or nil if does not.\n+\tScoreNormalizer() ScoreNormalizer\n }\n```\n\n### 2. pkg/scheduler/metrics/metrics.go\n\n**Changes:**\n- Rename metric constant from `ScoreExtensionNormalize` to `ScoreNormalize`\n\n```diff\n \tPreFilterExtensionAddPod    = \"PreFilterExtensionAddPod\"\n \tPreFilterExtensionRemovePod = \"PreFilterExtensionRemovePod\"\n \tPostFilter                  = \"PostFilter\"\n \tPreScore                    = \"PreScore\"\n \tScore                       = \"Score\"\n-\tScoreExtensionNormalize     = \"ScoreExtensionNormalize\"\n+\tScoreNormalize              = \"ScoreNormalize\"\n \tPreBind                     = \"PreBind\"\n \tBind                        = \"Bind\"\n \tPostBind                    = \"PostBind\"\n```\n\n### 3. pkg/scheduler/framework/runtime/framework.go\n\n**Changes:**\n- Update all references to `ScoreExtensions()` to `ScoreNormalizer()`\n- Update metric constant reference from `ScoreExtensionNormalize` to `ScoreNormalize`\n\n```diff\n \t// Run NormalizeScore method for each ScorePlugin in parallel.\n \tf.Parallelizer().Until(ctx, len(plugins), func(index int) {\n \t\tpl := plugins[index]\n-\t\tif pl.ScoreExtensions() == nil {\n+\t\tif pl.ScoreNormalizer() == nil {\n \t\t\treturn\n \t\t}\n \t\tnodeScoreList := pluginToNodeScores[pl.Name()]\n \t\tstatus := f.runScoreExtension(ctx, pl, state, pod, nodeScoreList)\n \t\t...\n \t}, metrics.Score)\n\n func (f *frameworkImpl) runScoreExtension(ctx context.Context, pl framework.ScorePlugin, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeScoreList framework.NodeScoreList) *framework.Status {\n \tif !state.ShouldRecordPluginMetrics() {\n-\t\treturn pl.ScoreExtensions().NormalizeScore(ctx, state, pod, nodeScoreList)\n+\t\treturn pl.ScoreNormalizer().NormalizeScore(ctx, state, pod, nodeScoreList)\n \t}\n \tstartTime := time.Now()\n-\tstatus := pl.ScoreExtensions().NormalizeScore(ctx, state, pod, nodeScoreList)\n-\tf.metricsRecorder.ObservePluginDurationAsync(metrics.ScoreExtensionNormalize, pl.Name(), status.Code().String(), metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\n+\tstatus := pl.ScoreNormalizer().NormalizeScore(ctx, state, pod, nodeScoreList)\n+\tf.metricsRecorder.ObservePluginDurationAsync(metrics.ScoreNormalize, pl.Name(), status.Code().String(), metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\n \treturn status\n }\n```\n\n### 4-13. Plugin Implementation Files\n\nFor each plugin implementation, rename the method from `ScoreExtensions()` to `ScoreNormalizer()` and update the return type annotation from `framework.ScoreExtensions` to `framework.ScoreNormalizer`.\n\nExample pattern (applies to all plugins):\n\n```diff\n-// ScoreExtensions of the Score plugin.\n-func (pl *PluginName) ScoreExtensions() framework.ScoreExtensions {\n+// ScoreNormalizer of the Score plugin.\n+func (pl *PluginName) ScoreNormalizer() framework.ScoreNormalizer {\n \t// return nil or return pl (if implementing the interface)\n }\n```\n\n**Affected files:**\n1. pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/noderesources/fit.go\n2. pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/interpodaffinity/scoring.go\n3. pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/podtopologyspread/scoring.go\n4. pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/nodeaffinity/node_affinity.go\n5. pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/volumebinding/volume_binding.go\n6. pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/imagelocality/image_locality.go\n7. pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/tainttoleration/taint_toleration.go\n8. pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/noderesources/balanced_allocation.go\n9. pkg/scheduler/testing/framework/fake_plugins.go\n10. pkg/scheduler/testing/framework/fake_extender.go\n\n### 14-16. Any external plugins that implement the `ScorePlugin` interface must be updated.\n\n**Scope**: The change is well-contained within the scheduler framework package and its plugins. No cross-subsystem dependencies.\n\n**Testing**: The change must be validated by:\n- Unit tests for the affected plugins (ensure they still implement the interface correctly)\n- Integration tests for the scheduling workflow (ensure score normalization still works)\n- Metrics tests (ensure the renamed constant is properly recorded)\n\n### Verification Approach\n\n1. **Compilation**: The changes should compile without errors once all references are updated\n2. **Type Safety**: Go's type system ensures that all implementations match the interface signature\n3. **Tests**: Existing unit and integration tests verify that the functionality is preserved\n4. **Search for References**: A complete grep for \"ScoreExtensions\" and \"ScoreExtensionNormalize\" should return no results after refactoring\n\n## Implementation Status\n\n### Completed Files (Successfully Created/Modified)\n\n**Core Definition Files:**\n- ✅ `/workspace/pkg/scheduler/framework/interface.go` - Interface and
